CPA License Revocation—A formal administrative disciplinary order issued by the Accounting Examining Board permanently revoking a Certified Public Accountant's registration following a conviction for a financial felony, gross negligence in asset audits, or intentional balance sheet fraud.
anchors.subcategory: Professional Licensing Discipline & Institutional Welfare Actions

anchors.phrase: Active Felony Wage Fraud Warrant—An open, judicially executed arrest warrant commanding law enforcement officers to locate and physically apprehend an employer, company executive, or corporate officer indicted for high-tier felony wage theft or fraudulent non-payment of earnings.

Financial Exploitation of a Vulnerable Adult—An individual in a position of trust, confidence, or fiduciary authority intentionally taking, stealing, obtaining, or using the property, cash, or financial assets of an elder or vulnerable adult with intent to defraud and deprive them of their estate value.

Criminal Tax Evasion—Willfully attempting in any manner to evade or defeat any tax imposed under Chapter 71, or filing completely fabricated or fraudulent income, corporate, or franchise tax returns with intent to defraud the state.

Active Fiduciary Fraud Warrant—An open, judicially executed arrest warrant commanding law enforcement officers to locate and physically apprehend an individual, fiduciary agent, or legal guardian indicted for high-tier felony financial exploitation of an elder or vulnerable adult.

Attempted Tax Evasion—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to evade or defeat state income or corporate franchise taxes by creating fraudulent transactional ledgers, which are intercepted, detected, or audited prior to final tax submission draw.

Antitrust Restraint of Trade—Intentionally engaging in a conspiracy, trust, or combination in restraint of trade or commerce, including horizontal price-fixing, market allocation, or collaborative bid-rigging schemes on public or private procurement contracts.

Public Contract Kickback—An independent contractor, vendor, or public agent offering, giving, soliciting, or receiving any unpermitted rebate, percentage compensation, or kickback return as an inducement to influence or steer the award of a public works or municipal procurement contract.
anchors.subcategory: Bribery of a Public Servant / Official

anchors.phrase: Public Vendor Procurement Debarment—An administrative declaration of non-responsibility or formal exclusion issued by the Department of Administration (DOA) debarring a contractor or company from bidding on public contracts following a state antitrust conviction, systemic wage theft history, or matching federal SAM exclusion listings.

Attempted Restraint of Trade—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to establish an anti-competitive price-fixing or bid-rigging ring, which are detected, reported by corporate compliance audits, or intercepted by state investigators prior to the final competitive public bid opening.

Boating Under the Influence (1st Offense)—Operating a motorboat or manipulating water skis, aquaplanes, or similar devices on the navigable waters of the state while under the influence of an intoxicant, or with a prohibited alcohol concentration of 0.08% or more.

Criminal BUI (2nd Offense)—Operating a motorboat or manipulating water skis, aquaplanes, or similar devices on navigable waters within 5 years of a prior OWI, BUI, or chemical test refusal conviction.

Maritime Implied Consent Refusal—An operator of a motorboat intentionally refusing to submit to a lawful chemical test of their breath, blood, or urine requested by a law enforcement officer or DNR warden who has probable cause to arrest the person for boating under the influence.

Attempted Criminal BUI—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to start, navigate, or operate a motorboat on navigable waters while under the influence of an intoxicant, where the attempt executes at a repeat offender level (2nd or subsequent track) and is intercepted or halted by law enforcement or DNR wardens prior to successful watercraft locomotion.

Bribery of Athletic Participant—Intentionally offering, promising, or giving anything of value to any participant, official, coach, or manager in any athletic contest, match, or sporting event with intent to influence them to lose, limit the margin of victory, or cheat to manipulate sports wagering outcomes.

Attempted Commercial Gambling—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to open, operate, or finance an illegal bookmaking or commercial card enterprise, which are halted by law enforcement or regulatory sweeps prior to the execution of active betting operations or card pool mechanics.

Petty Gambling (Municipal)—Participating in an unlicensed raffle, local poker pool, or street dice game, or possessing nominal unpermitted video gaming mechanisms within municipal limits below state commercial tracking ceilings.

Substantiated Child Abuse/Neglect—A final administrative determination by a county Child Protective Services (CPS) agency or DCF panel confirming, by a preponderance of the evidence, that an individual committed physical abuse, sexual abuse, emotional damage, or severe neglect against a child.

Substantiated Caregiver Misconduct—A final administrative determination by a DHS panel confirming, by a preponderance of the evidence, that an individual employed in a state-regulated entity committed abuse, financial exploitation, neglect, or misappropriation of property against a vulnerable adult or nursing home resident.

Capias / Warrant for Failure to Appear (CPS Nexus)—A judicially issued bench warrant or capias commanding law enforcement to physically apprehend a parent, guardian, or respondent who failed to appear in court for an active child abuse fact-finding or Child in Need of Protection or Services (CHIPS) proceeding.

Criminal Damage to Property (Felony Tier)—Intentionally causing physical damage to the non-residential, commercial, or public property of another without consent, where the reduction in value or cost of repair exceeds the felony statutory threshold.

Unlawful Tenant Holdover—A tenant failing to pay rent, violating material lease terms, or refusing to vacate a rental property after receiving a formal statutory 5-day, 14-day, or 28-day written notice to quit or remedy, causing an eviction action to be filed and adjudicated.

Active Writ of Restitution—A judicially issued execution order commanding the county sheriff to locate, physically remove an unlawful holdover occupant from a designated parcel, and seize or secure remaining property assets to satisfy outstanding landlord judgments.
Manslaughter
Battery in the First Degree
Battery in the Second Degree
Battery in the Third Degree
Terroristic Threatening in the First Degree
Sexual Indecency with a Child – Engaging in conduct that is sexual in nature in the presence of a child for the purpose of arousing or gratifying the sexual desire of any person.
Sexual Assault in the First Degree
Sexual Assault in the Second Degree
Sexual Assault in the Third Degree
Sexual Assault in the Fourth Degree
Stalking in the First Degree
Stalking in the Second Degree
Theft of Property – First Degree ($25,000 or more)
Theft of Property – Second Degree ($5,000–$24,999)
Theft of Property – Third Degree ($1,000–$4,999)
Theft of Property – Fourth Degree (under $1,000)
Burglary (General / Breaking or Entering)
Criminal Mischief in the First Degree
Criminal Mischief in the Second Degree
Forgery in the First Degree
Forgery in the Second Degree
Unlawful Deepfake Distribution
Failure to Appear – Willful failure to appear before a court as required by a lawful order or citation; penal code track; classified based on severity of underlying offense.
Bail Jumping – Failing to appear as required after being released on bail or recognizance; criminal procedure enforcement track; separate charge that may run concurrently with underlying offense.
Escape in the First Degree
Escape in the Second Degree
Furnishing Contraband to Inmate
Possession of Firearm by Felon
Delivery / Manufacture of Methamphetamine or Cocaine
Violation of Order of Protection – Knowingly violating a valid court-issued civil or criminal order of protection.
Domestic Abuse Definitions – Pure definition section; contains no standalone criminal or civil offenses.
Child Maltreatment Findings – Definition framework for administrative/civil adjudication of abuse.
Child Care Facility Registry Violations – Operating or working in a child care facility with a disqualifying registry entry.
Professional Engineers – Fraud, deceit, gross negligence, or misconduct in engineering practice.
Contractor Violations – Working as a commercial contractor without a valid state license.
Contractor Civil Penalties – Committing residential contracting violations or failing to maintain a bond.
Nursing License Discipline – Fraud in procuring license, drug diversion, gross incompetence, or unfitness.
Medical Board Actions – Unprofessional conduct, gross negligence, or unauthorized prescription practices.
Pharmacy License Discipline – Professional misconduct, substitution of drugs, or violating drug safety laws.
Child Care Civil Penalties – Violating statutory child care standards or safety directives.
Abuse Reporting – Willful failure of a mandated reporter to report suspected child/adult abuse.
Food Establishment Violations – Manufacturing, selling, or holding adulterated or misbranded food.
Retail Food Permit – Operating a food facility without or in violation of an active health permit.
Waste Hauler Violations – Illegal dumping or transporting solid waste without a regional board authority license.
Environmental Civil Penalties – General administrative violations of pollution controls or board orders.
Hazardous Waste Violations – Unlawful storage, transport, treatment, or disposal of regulated hazardous waste.
Water Pollution Enforcement – Discharging unpermitted pollutants into waters of the state.
Constitutional Disqualifier – Absolute constitutional bar for those convicted of infamous crimes/felonies from holding public office.
Bribery/Public Corruption – Offering, giving, or receiving unlawful benefit to alter official action.
anchors.subcategory: Bribery of a Public Servant / Official

anchors.phrase: Election Disqualification – Committing voter fraud, campaign finance fraud, or illegal voting acts.
Public Office Ineligibility Findings – Judicial or legislative finding of malfeasance or constitutional default.
Driving While Suspended – Operating a motor vehicle while state driving privileges are actively suspended.
Failure to Appear (Traffic) – Failing to respond to a signed traffic citation or court summons.
CDL Disqualification – Violations triggering mandatory CDL suspension (e.g., DUI in commercial vehicle).
Vehicle Registration Suspension – Operating a vehicle with a registration suspended for insurance lapses.
Child Abuse Registry Findings – True administrative finding of child abuse/neglect by preponderance of evidence.
Adult Maltreatment Registry Findings – True administrative finding of abuse/exploitation of an endangered adult.
Professional Misconduct Findings – Formal determination of rule or ethics violation by a state licensing board.
Healthcare Exclusion Registry – Adjudication triggering inclusion on federal OIG exclusion list.
Medicaid Exclusion Actions – Program fraud, billing abuse, or kickbacks in Medicaid delivery.
Licensing Board Final Orders – Issuance of final disciplinary orders following a board hearing.
Attorney Discipline – Violations of the Rules of Professional Conduct, trust account fraud, or felony convictions.
Real Estate Licensing – Practicing without a license, misrepresentation, or mishandling earnest money.
Insurance Producer Discipline – Fraudulent insurance practices, premium diversion, or material misrepresentation.
CPA Discipline – Fraud in obtaining certificate, gross negligence, or dishonesty in financial audits.
Educator Licensure Discipline – Felony convictions, child maltreatment findings, test security violations, or ethical breaches.
Federal OIG LEIE – Mandatory or permissive exclusion due to Medicare/Medicaid fraud, patient abuse, or health-related felonies.
NPDB Reporting – Malpractice payments, clinical privilege restrictions, or professional society actions.
Arkansas Medicaid Exclusion – Fraud, programmatic overbilling, or provider integrity defaults in state programs.
State Police Employment Disqualifiers – Felony convictions, domestic violence convictions, or patterns of misconduct.
Childcare Employment Disqualifiers – True maltreatment findings, violent crimes, or sexual offenses barring childcare employment.
Long-Term Care Worker Exclusions – Convictions for abuse, neglect, exploitation, or theft from vulnerable adults.
School Employee Background Disqualifiers – Disqualifying offenses including sexual misconduct, violence, or drug distribution.
Child Maltreatment Central Registry – True administrative finding of child abuse/neglect.
Adult Maltreatment Central Registry – True finding of abuse, neglect, or exploitation of vulnerable adults.
Sex Offender Registry – Criminal conviction for a qualifying sexual or kidnapping offense.
OIG Exclusion List – Federal healthcare program exclusions or health fraud convictions.
NURSYS – State nursing board final disciplinary orders/actions reported to national database.
NPDB – Professional malpractice payouts and disciplinary actions reported to national practitioner roster.
AR Contractor Registry – Formal licensing board citations and code violations logged in state compliance system.
Production of Child Pornography – Employing, using, or persuading a minor to engage in sexually explicit conduct for visual recording.
Possession of Child Pornography – Knowingly possessing, viewing, or downloading visual depictions of a minor engaging in sexually explicit conduct.
Sexual Grooming of a Minor – Using electronic or physical communication to induce a minor to engage in illicit sexual activity.
Trafficking of Persons (Sexual) – Recruiting, harboring, or transporting a person for the purpose of commercial sex through force, fraud, or coercion.
Nonconsensual Sexual Recording (Revenge Porn) – Knowingly distributing intimate images of another person without consent to cause harm or distress.
EMS Discipline – Gross negligence, practicing beyond scope, or drug diversion by an EMT/Paramedic.
Psychology License Discipline – Professional misconduct, dual relationships, fraud, or practicing without a license. Regulated by the Arkansas Psychology Board.

Licensed Professional Counselor / MFT License Discipline – Professional misconduct, dual relationships, fraud, or practicing without a license. Regulated by the Arkansas Board of Examiners in Counseling (LPCs, Marriage & Family Therapists).
Long-Term Care Facility Enforcement – Facility failure to maintain safety standards resulting in resident injury or neglect.
Substance Abuse Treatment Licensing – Operating an uncertified rehabilitation center or violating state methadone dispensing rules.
Underground Storage Tanks (UST) – Operating an unregistered tank, failing to report leaks, or bypassing fuel delivery red tags.
Air Permitting Specifics – Constructing or operating a stationary air emission source without or in violation of a Title V permit.
Asbestos Licensing – Performing asbestos abatement or demolition without a license, supervisor certificate, or proper wet-method disposal.

Environmental Remediation – Voluntary Clean-Up Program (VCP/Brownfields) – Intentionally falsifying site assessments or remedial reports within the state voluntary cleanup track; DEQ Brownfields program.

Environmental Remediation – Petroleum Storage Tank (PST) Program – Falsifying or failing to complete required remediation reports under the state Petroleum Storage Tank cleanup authority; separate DEQ regulatory track from VCP.
Federal SAM Exclusions – Broad administrative inclusion based on systemic fraud, default, or bad-faith contract performance.
Federal Debarment (FAR 9.4) – Formal administrative determination that a contractor is non-responsible due to a criminal conviction or serious civil breach.
Federal Suspension (FAR 9.4) – Temporary exclusion of a contractor pending the completion of an investigation or legal proceeding based on indictments or adequate evidence.
FINRA Bars – Failure to comply with requests for information (Rule 8210), insider trading, conversion of funds, or market manipulation.
SEC Administrative Orders – Serious violations of federal securities law, fraudulent disclosure schemes, or underlying financial felonies.
Investment Adviser Discipline – Breaching fiduciary duties, misleading advisory clients, or running hidden fee schemes.
NMLS Enforcement Actions – Failing to disclose material criminal histories, escrow account skimming, or deceptive loan packaging.
Mortgage Loan Originator (MLO) Revocations – Statutory disqualification resulting from a financial felony or a pattern of regulatory consumer fraud.
GSA SAM Exclusions – General non-procurement or procurement defaults; systemic structural compliance failures.
HUD Debarments – Violating the Fair Housing Act, equity diversion, or construction kickbacks on HUD-backed projects.
USDA Exclusions – Falsifying agricultural loan logs, food safety defaults, or fraud in federal nutrition assistance programs.
Tribal Court Civil/Criminal Orders – Domestic violence protection order violations or civil tort adjudications inside tribal boundaries.
UCMJ Convictions – Court-martial convictions for violent acts, theft, espionage, drug distribution, or conduct unbecoming.
Computer Fraud – Accessing a computer, system, or network to devise a scheme to defraud or obtain money/property.
Identity Theft – Knowingly using or attempting to use personal identifying information of another person without consent for financial gain.
Unregistered Securities Sales – Offering or selling any investment security in Arkansas unless registered, exempt, or a federal covered security.
Miscellaneous Election Fraud – Tampering with voting machines, ballot box stuffing, or falsifying voter registration materials.
Unlawful Use of Official Position – A public official using their office to secure special privileges or exemptions for personal financial gain.
Failure to File Statement of Financial Interest – Willful failure of a candidate or designated public official to file required asset/income disclosure forms.
Juvenile Transfer to Adult Court – Statutory mechanism allowing or forcing a juvenile (14 or older) to be tried as an adult for capital, violent, or Class Y/A/B felonies.
Confidentiality & Record Sealing – Unlawful disclosure of confidential juvenile court records, or rules governing the automatic sealing of delinquency files.
Probation – Court-ordered supervision in lieu of or following incarceration; conditions may include reporting, drug testing, curfew, and community service.
Probation Revocation – Court proceeding to revoke suspended sentence or probation upon violation of supervision conditions.
Parole – Conditional supervised release from incarceration prior to completion of full sentence; subject to Board of Corrections approval.
Parole Revocation – Administrative or judicial proceeding to revoke parole upon violation of release conditions.
Good Time / Earned Time Credits – Sentence reduction mechanism for compliant institutional behavior; reduces time served toward parole eligibility.
Community Correction Center Transfer – Residential placement in a community correction center as alternative to or transition from state prison incarceration.

Escape / Absconding from Supervision – Criminal charge for willfully failing to return to custody or absconding from supervised release conditions; penal code track; charged as a standalone criminal offense.

Supervision Revocation Procedure – Administrative and judicial process for revoking probation, parole, or community supervision upon violation of conditions; criminal procedure track; not a standalone criminal charge but appears on court dockets as a revocation proceeding.
Electronic Monitoring – Court- or board-ordered GPS/electronic monitoring as a condition of release, supervision, or alternative to incarceration.
Work Release – Supervised employment program allowing incarcerated individuals to work in the community during daytime hours while returning to facility nightly.
Work Release Violation – Failure to return to facility, job loss, or other breach of work release conditions.
Record Sealing – Statutory process to seal (restrict public access to) eligible criminal records after completion of sentence and applicable waiting period.
Expungement – Process to physically destroy or set aside eligible criminal records; more complete than sealing; treated as if the offense never occurred for most purposes.
Governor's Pardon – Executive clemency restoring civil rights and forgiving the offense; does not expunge the record but removes legal disqualifications associated with the conviction.
Restoration of Voting Rights – Process by which persons convicted of felonies regain the right to vote upon completion of sentence including any supervision period.
Restoration of Firearm Rights – State and federal processes for restoring the right to possess firearms after a felony conviction; state restoration does not automatically restore federal rights.
Juvenile Record Sealing – Automatic or petition-based sealing of juvenile court adjudication records; prevents juvenile record from appearing on standard adult background checks.
DNA Record Expungement – Process to expunge DNA records from the Arkansas State DNA Database upon acquittal, dismissal, or reversal of conviction.
Effect of Sealed Record on Employment – Statutory provisions governing what employers can and cannot ask or access regarding sealed criminal records.
Administrative Record Correction – Process to correct factual errors in criminal history records maintained by Arkansas Crime Information Center (ACIC) or other state databases.
Employer Access to Sealed Records – Statutory framework governing which employers and agencies retain lawful access to sealed criminal records notwithstanding sealing order.
Certificate of Rehabilitation – Court-issued document demonstrating rehabilitation; may mitigate collateral consequences for employment and licensing even where full expungement is unavailable.
Reckless Driving – Driving in willful or wanton disregard for the safety of persons or property.
Leaving Scene of Accident (Hit and Run) – Failing to stop and remain at the scene of an accident involving injury or death.
anchors.subcategory: Hit and Run — Property Damage Only (Misdemeanor)

anchors.phrase: Fleeing by Vehicle – Purposely fleeing in a motor vehicle from a law enforcement officer signaling to stop.
Implied Consent / Refusal – Refusing to submit to a chemical test of breath, blood, or urine for DWI detection.
Drug Paraphernalia – Possessing items to plant, propagate, cultivate, harvest, pack, store, or inject drugs.
Maintaining Drug Premises – Knowingly keeping/maintaining a structure, vehicle, or facility used for drug activities.
Simultaneous Possession – Possessing a firearm while simultaneously possessing a felony quantity of drugs.
Doctor Shopping / Rx Fraud – Obtaining controlled substances by fraud, deceit, misrepresentation, or concealment.
Counterfeit Substances – Creating, delivering, or possessing with intent to deliver a counterfeit substance.
Meth Lab Equipment – Possessing equipment, products, or materials with intent to manufacture methamphetamine; items include pseudoephedrine, glassware, tubing, or other lab apparatus.

Precursor Chemicals (Anhydrous Ammonia) – Possessing anhydrous ammonia with intent to manufacture methamphetamine; includes unlawful storage, transport, or transfer of anhydrous ammonia in non-approved containers.
Drug-Free Zone Enhancements – Manufacturing, delivering, or possessing drugs within 1,000 feet of schools, parks, or youth centers.
Delivery to Minors – An adult delivering a controlled substance to a minor who is at least 3 years younger.
Fentanyl Enhancements – Mandatory sentencing enhancements for trafficking or causing death with fentanyl.
Domestic Battering – Purposely causing physical injury to a family or household member (1st, 2nd, 3rd Degree).

Aggravated Assault on a Family or Household Member – Purposely engaging in conduct, displaying a firearm, or strangling/impeding respiration under circumstances manifesting extreme indifference to human life.
Strangulation Enhancements – Impeding normal breathing or circulation by applying pressure to the throat or neck.

Endangering the welfare of a minor in the first degree – Knowingly engaging in conduct creating a substantial risk of death or serious physical injury to a minor, or causing or permitting a minor to be exposed to a controlled substance. Processed exclusively on the Title 5 criminal penal track; operationally insulated from Title 9 DHS administrative maltreatment adjudications (Child Maltreatment Act, § 12-18-101 et seq.) which govern civil registry findings. A criminal conviction under this section does not merge with and is not substituted by an administrative maltreatment substantiation.
anchors.subcategory: Endangering the Welfare of a Minor

anchors.phrase: Elder / Vulnerable Adult Abuse – Knowingly abusing, neglecting, exploiting, or causing physical or mental injury to an endangered or impaired adult; Title 5 Chapter 28 — distinct victim class and distinct elements from child statutes.
Straw Purchase – Purchasing a firearm on behalf of a person known to be prohibited from owning one.
Defaced Firearms – Knowingly possessing, selling, or receiving a firearm with a removed or altered serial number.
Machine Gun Offenses – Possessing or using a machine gun for an aggressive or offensive purpose.
Possession on School Property – Possessing a firearm upon the property of any public school, college, or university campus.
Unlawful Discharge – Discharging a firearm from a vehicle (drive-by shooting) or across a public highway.
Money Laundering – Conducting financial transactions with proceeds of crime to conceal their nature or source.
Securities Fraud – Employing a device, scheme, or artifice to defraud in connection with the sale of securities.
Insurance Fraud (Criminal) – Knowingly presenting false claims or statements to an insurer for financial payout.
Cyber Fraud / Computer Crimes – Accessing a computer system or network to execute a scheme to defraud or extort.
Speeding – General moving infraction; failure to drive at a speed reasonable and prudent under traffic conditions.
Use of Handheld Wireless Device in School Zone – Operating a handheld wireless device while driving in a designated school zone.
Overtaking or Passing a Stopped School Bus – Passing a school bus with flashing red lights and stop arm extended; mandatory fine with license suspension.
Careless Driving – Operating a vehicle without due care or attention; distinct from Reckless Driving (§ 27-51-104) which requires willful/wanton disregard.

Fraudulent Practices in Securities (Administrative Track) – Administrative enforcement action for employing a fraudulent scheme or device in connection with securities; civil track distinct from the criminal § 23-42-501 felony track.
Theft of Services – Intentionally obtaining services by deception, threat, coercion, or without authorization; scaled by value of services.

Aggravated assault upon a law enforcement officer or an employee of a correctional facility – Purposely expelling bodily fluids (saliva, blood, urine, feces) onto an officer/employee, or knowingly discharging a firearm at them within the scope of their official duties.
Knowingly Furnishing Alcohol to a Minor – Providing, purchasing, or procuring alcohol for a person under 21 years of age; escalates to felony on subsequent offense.
Coercion – Compelling a person to engage in or abstain from conduct by threat of injury or accusation.
Hazing – Engaging in abusive behavior as a condition of initiation into an organization or student group.
Non-Support – Willful failure to provide mandatory support to a child or spouse as ordered by a court.
Criminal Possession of Explosives – Possessing or manufacturing an explosive device with intent to use unlawfully.
Use of Weapon in Commission of Crime – Mandatory firearm enhancement for committing a felony with a firearm.
anchors.subcategory: Use of Weapon in Commission of Crime

anchors.phrase: Criminal Trespass – Entering or remaining unlawfully upon the premises of another.
Arkansas Racketeer Influenced and Corrupt Organizations Act (RICO)
Encouraging Suicide – Intentionally advising, encouraging, or otherwise causing another person to commit or attempt to commit suicide.
Sexual Indecency with a Child
Embezzlement – Consolidated theft of fiduciary property; theft by a person entrusted with another's property.
Fraudulent Business Practices – Knowingly making false material statements or using deceptive practices in the course of business transactions.
Obstructing Justice – Hindering the apprehension or prosecution of another person known to have committed an offense.
Contempt of Court – Willful disobedience of a court order or disruption of judicial proceedings.
Commercial Driver License DUI / DWI (BAC 0.04%) – CDL holder operating a commercial vehicle with blood alcohol at or above 0.04%; mandatory disqualification track.
Operation of Aircraft Under Influence of Alcohol or Drugs – Operating any aircraft in Arkansas while under the influence of alcohol or a controlled substance.
Utility Theft or Signal Piracy – Unauthorized connection to or diversion of electric; gas; water; cable; or telecommunications services.
Procurement Fraud / Corporate Debarment (Administrative Track) – Bidding collusion; false certification; or fraudulent performance under an Arkansas state procurement contract.
Weights and Measures Violations – Commercial scale or measuring device tampering; using non-certified devices in commercial trade.
Labor Law Violations (Wage Theft / Minimum Wage Infractions) – Employers failing to pay minimum wage; overtime; or prevailing wage as required by Arkansas law.
Sabotage or Insurrection
Criminal Conspiracy
Educator Licensure Disqualification
Boating Under the Influence (BUI)
Gambling / Keeping a Gambling House
Harassment (Hate Crimes & Civil Rights Context)
Tax Evasion / Fraud
Burglary (Property & Housing Protections)
Treason
Employment of Unauthorized Aliens

Indecent Exposure – Purposely exposing genitals under circumstances in which the person knows the conduct is likely to cause affront or alarm. Applies to adult victims; does not require a minor victim. Distinct from § 5-14-110 (Sexual Indecency with a Child), which governs conduct specifically targeting persons under 15 years of age.
Assisted Suicide — Intentionally or knowingly providing the physical means or participating in a physical act assisting another to end their life.
First Degree Murder — Premeditated and intentional killing of another; or a killing resulting from a destructive device/bomb.
Inclusion of an Unborn Child — Statutory provision directing that any criminal homicide (Murder, Manslaughter) applies to an unborn child at any stage of gestation.
Reckless Homicide — The reckless killing of another individual (Serves as Tennessee's statutory replacement for involuntary manslaughter).
Assisted Suicide (Medical Provider / Ancillary Focus) — Explicit criminalization of assisting a suicide, functioning as a direct bar against physician-assisted dying frameworks.
Reckless Homicide — Consciously disregarding a substantial and unjustifiable risk resulting in another person's death.
Second Degree Murder — A knowing killing of another; or a killing resulting from the unlawful distribution of Schedule I or II drugs.
First Degree Murder; intentional/premeditated killing, or felony murder during enumerated crimes (theft, rape, etc.)
Second Degree Murder; the knowing killing of another, or death resulting from unlawful drug distribution.
Voluntary Manslaughter; intentional or knowing killing committed in a state of passion produced by adequate provocation.
Reckless Homicide; the reckless killing of another person. (Tennessee's functional framework for traditional involuntary manslaughter).
Criminally Negligent Homicide; criminally negligent conduct that results in death. (Third leg of Tennessee's involuntary manslaughter model).
Vehicular Homicide; killing of another via motor vehicle operation, drug/alcohol intoxication, or drag racing.
Aggravated Vehicular Homicide; vehicular homicide combined with prior DUI convictions, prior vehicular homicide convictions, or an extreme blood alcohol concentration level.

Felony Murder; a killing committed in the perpetration of, or attempt to perpetrate, any first degree murder, act of terrorism, arson, burglary, theft, kidnapping, robbery, extortion, rape, or escape.
Criminally Negligent Homicide; criminally negligent conduct that proximately causes the death of an individual.
Inclusions for Homicide; establishes that the terms "another," "individuals," and "another person" include a human fetus at any stage of gestation in utero for any criminal homicide prosecution.
Voluntary Manslaughter; the intentional or knowing killing of another in a state of passion produced by adequate provocation sufficient to lead a reasonable person to act in an irrational manner.
Reckless Homicide; the reckless killing of another individual, where the offender is aware of but consciously disregards a substantial and unjustifiable risk that death will result.

Vehicular Homicide; the reckless killing of another by the operation of an automobile, airplane, motorboat or other motor vehicle, as the proximate result of conduct creating a substantial risk of death or serious bodily injury, drag racing, or driver intoxication (DUI).

Aggravated Vehicular Homicide; committing vehicular homicide under § 39-13-213(a)(2) where the defendant has two or more prior convictions for DUI or vehicular assault, one prior conviction for vehicular homicide, or a blood alcohol concentration (BAC) of 0.20% or more at the time of the offense combined with one prior DUI/vehicular assault conviction.

Assisted Suicide; intentionally providing another person with the physical means to commit suicide or intentionally participating in a physical act by which another person commits suicide, with knowledge of the person's intent to commit suicide.
Vandalism / Damage to Agricultural Property; knowingly causing damage, destruction, or monetary loss to agricultural commodities, machinery, or farm operations.
Unlawful Acquisition of Agricultural Land by a Prohibited Foreign Party; prohibited foreign entities or businesses holding interest in Tennessee agricultural land.
Theft of Services / Damaging Utilities; bypassing, altering, or tampering with agricultural irrigation meters, lines, or public water conduits to unlawfully divert water for crop use.

Violation of Livestock Market Quarantine Orders; selling, consigning, or moving livestock known to be infected with or exposed to communicable diseases out of an active quarantine perimeter without permission from the State Veterinarian.

Commercial Weights and Measures Deception; using, selling, or maintaining an incorrect weighing or measuring device for direct commercial sales, or misrepresenting the quantity of an agricultural commodity.
Vandalism or damage to agricultural property, where the total value of the destroyed commodities, crops, or farm equipment is $1,000 or less.
Vandalism or damage to agricultural property, where the total value of the destroyed commodities, crops, fields, livestock herds, or complex machinery is $250,000 or more.
Theft of services or utility tampering; unlawfully bypassing, altering, or tapping into agricultural irrigation lines or meters where the stolen water value is $1,000 or less.
Theft of services or utility tampering; large-scale unauthorized diversion, pumping, or meter manipulation of public/commercial irrigation infrastructure where the stolen value is $250,000 or more.
Commodity Warehouse and Grain Dealer Deception; issuing fraudulent warehouse receipts, scaling deceptive volumes, or bribing public agricultural inspectors to misrepresent grain quality grading.
Seed Label Counterfeiting and Fraud; intentionally mislabeling agricultural seeds, distributing toxic or weed-infested seed mixes, or falsifying germinating data certifications to growers.

Industrial Hemp Regulation Violations; cultivating industrial hemp without an active Department of Agriculture license, failing to destroy crops testing over the legal delta-9 THC limit, or forging chain-of-custody botanical transit manifests.

Concealment of Communicable Livestock Disease; intentionally hiding or failing to report outbreaks of scheduled livestock diseases (such as Anthrax, Brucellosis, or Avian Influenza) to the State Veterinarian, or resisting inspection teams.
Cruelty to Animals; depriving commercial livestock herds of necessary food, water, or shelter, or intentionally torturing animals within an agricultural processing facility.

Failure to Maintain Timber Purchase Records; sawmill operators or timber buyers failing to secure and preserve a valid bill of sale or evidence of legal ownership from the seller for at least one year.
Critical Infrastructure Vandalism; knowingly destroying, injuring, or interfering with a farm, including the crops, animals, real property, or equipment contained on that farm.

Aggravated Cruelty to Livestock Animals; intentionally or knowingly torturing, burning, or killing a livestock animal without the effective consent of the owner or outside accepted veterinary practices.
Timber Theft; exercising unauthorized control over standing timber where the stumpage market value is $1,000 or less.
Timber Theft; unauthorized cutting/control over standing timber valued between $1,001 and $2,499.
Timber Theft; unauthorized cutting/control over standing timber valued between $2,500 and $9,999.
Timber Theft; unauthorized cutting/control over standing timber valued between $10,000 and $59,999.
Timber Theft; large-scale commercial unauthorized cutting/control over standing timber valued between $60,000 and $249,999.
Timber Theft; catastrophic large-scale corporate timber poaching operation valued at $250,000 or more.
Simple Assault; intentionally, knowingly or recklessly causing bodily injury to another person.
Simple Assault by Threat; intentionally or knowingly causing another to reasonably fear imminent bodily injury.
Simple Assault by Offensive Contact; intentionally or knowingly causing physical contact with another and a reasonable person would regard the contact as extremely offensive or provocative.
Aggravated Assault; intentionally or knowingly committing an assault as defined in § 39-13-101(a)(1) that results in serious bodily injury to another.
Aggravated Assault with a Weapon; intentionally or knowingly committing an assault as defined in § 39-13-101 that involves the use or display of a deadly weapon.
Aggravated Assault by Omission; intentionally or knowingly causing a child or vulnerable adult to suffer serious bodily injury or death through a pattern of failure to protect.

Aggravated Assault by Strangulation; intentionally or knowingly committing an assault as defined in § 39-13-101 that involves strangulation or attempted strangulation (impeding the normal breathing or blood circulation by applying pressure to the throat or neck).

Simple Assault by Contact (Tennessee's explicit Battery equivalent); intentionally or knowingly causing physical contact with another when a reasonable person would regard the contact as extremely offensive or provocative.
Aggravated Assault with Deadly Weapon; intentionally or knowingly committing an assault and displaying or using a deadly weapon.
Aggravated Assault by Strangulation (Tennessee's explicit Strangulation code entry); intentionally or knowingly committing an assault and causing multi-tier strangulation or suffocation.
Reckless Aggravated Assault; recklessly committing an assault and causing serious bodily injury to another.
Domestic Assault; committing an assault as defined in § 39-13-101 against a domestic abuse victim, including a current/former spouse, person cohabiting, dating partner, or relative by blood/marriage.

Reckless Endangerment with a Deadly Weapon; recklessly engaging in conduct that places or may place another person in imminent danger of death or serious bodily injury, committed with a deadly weapon.

Assault on an Emergency Medical Provider or First Responder; committing an assault as defined in § 39-13-101(a)(1) against an emergency medical technician, firefighter, or law enforcement officer while performing official duties.
Aggravated Kidnapping; false imprisonment as defined in § 39-13-302 committed to facilitate a felony, flight, or bodily injury; to terrorize; or while armed with a deadly weapon.

Especially Aggravated Kidnapping; knowingly removing or confining another person unlawfully, accomplished with a deadly weapon, where the victim suffers serious bodily injury, or where the victim is under 13 years old.
False Imprisonment; knowingly removing or confining another person unlawfully so as to interfere substantially with the person's liberty.

Hazing / Aggravated Hazing; any intentional or reckless act by a student or organization directed against any other student that endangers the mental or physical health or safety of the student for initiation or affiliation, resulting in serious bodily injury or extreme mental distress.

Malicious Wounding conductive mapping; Tennessee does not maintain a standalone statute named "Malicious Wounding". This conduct is completely absorbed, prosecuted, and classified under the statutory parameters of T.C.A. § 39-13-102(a)(1)(A)(i) (Aggravated Assault causing Serious Bodily Injury).
Coercion of a Witness; threatening a witness or prospective witness with injury or damage, or corruptly influencing a witness to testify falsely, withhold data, or elude official process.

Civil Rights Intimidation; injuring, damaging, threatening, or intimidating another person with the intent to interfere with the free exercise or enjoyment of any right secured by the constitution or laws, based on race, color, ancestry, religion, or national origin.

Hazing; any intentional or reckless act by a student or organization directed against any other student that endangers the mental or physical health or safety of the student for initiation or affiliation.
Aggravated Hazing; any intentional or reckless hazing act directed against any other student that results in serious bodily injury or extreme mental distress.
Aggravated Rape; unlawful, non-consensual sexual penetration accomplished with force/coercion where a weapon is used/displayed, the victim suffers serious injury, or multiple offenders are present.
Rape; unlawful, non-consensual sexual penetration accomplished by force or coercion, or where the victim is incapable of giving consent due to mental/physical impairment or intoxication.

Aggravated Robbery; the intentional or knowing theft of property from the person of another by violence or putting the person in fear, accomplished with a deadly weapon or where the victim suffers serious bodily injury.
Arson; knowingly damaging any structure by means of fire or explosion without the effective consent of all persons having a possessory, proprietary or security interest therein.
Aggravated Burglary; entering a habitation without the effective consent of the property owner with intent to commit a felony, theft, or assault.
Forgery; forging a document, signature, or commercial instrument with intent to defraud or harm another individual or business enterprise.

Identity Theft; knowingly obtaining, possessing, or using personal identifying information of another person with the intent to unlawfully obtain credit, goods, services, or property, or to commit any crime.
Forgery; forging or altering a document, check, or commercial instrument with intent to defraud, where the apparent value of the property or service involved is $1,000 or less.
Forgery; large-scale corporate check counterfeiting or document forgery where the apparent value of the property or service involved is $250,000 or more.
Perjury; making a false statement under oath or under penalty of perjury, with intent to deceive, during an official proceeding or on a required statutory document.

Bribery of a Public Servant; offering, conferring, or agreeing to confer any pecuniary benefit upon a public servant, or a public servant soliciting or accepting any benefit, to influence their official vote, opinion, judgment, or exercise of discretion.
anchors.subcategory: Bribery of a Public Servant / Official

anchors.phrase: Bail Jumping and Failure to Appear; intentionally failing to appear in court as directed by a lawful subpoena, citation, or release on bail condition relating to an active criminal charge.
Manufacture, delivery, sale, or possession with intent to manufacture, deliver, or sell a Schedule I controlled substance.
Manufacture, delivery, sale, or possession with intent to sell less than 0.5 grams of Cocaine, Methamphetamine, or a Schedule II narcotic substance.
Manufacture, delivery, sale, or possession with intent to sell 0.5 grams or more of Cocaine or Methamphetamine.
Manufacture, delivery, sale, or possession with intent to manufacture, deliver, or sell a Schedule III controlled substance (e.g., Anabolic Steroids, Ketamine).
Manufacture, delivery, sale, or possession with intent to manufacture, deliver, or sell a Schedule IV controlled substance (e.g., Xanax, Alprazolam, Clonazepam).
Manufacture, delivery, sale, or possession with intent to manufacture, deliver, or sell a Schedule V controlled substance (e.g., Codeine cough syrups in minor concentrations).
Manufacture, delivery, sale, or possession with intent to manufacture, deliver, or sell Marijuana in an amount less than one-half pound (0.5 lbs / 226 grams).
Manufacture, delivery, sale, or possession with intent to sell 300 grams or more of any substance containing Cocaine.

Simple Possession or Casual Exchange; knowingly possessing a small personal-use quantity of a controlled substance, or a casual exchange of a small amount without corporate/commercial distribution intent.
Simple Possession Recidivist Upgrade; possessing a personal-use quantity of a controlled substance after having two or more prior convictions for simple possession or casual exchange.
Unlawful Carrying of a Weapon; carrying a firearm or club with the intent to go armed, outside statutory open/concealed carry exceptions or permitless carry frameworks.
Felon in Possession of a Firearm; possessing a firearm after being convicted of a felony involving force, violence, or the use of a deadly weapon.
Felon in Possession of a Firearm (Drug Nexus); possessing a firearm after being convicted of a felony drug offense under Title 39, Chapter 17, Part 4.

Controlled Substance Manufacturing and Sale; intentionally manufacturing, delivering, selling, or possessing with intent to manufacture, deliver, or sell a Schedule I controlled substance (e.g., Heroin, LSD).

Controlled Substance Manufacturing and Sale; intentionally manufacturing, delivering, selling, or possessing with intent to manufacture, deliver, or sell a Schedule II controlled substance (e.g., Cocaine, Methamphetamine, Fentanyl, Oxycodone).

Possession of a Firearm While Intoxicated; possessing a firearm in a public place while under the influence of alcohol, any controlled substance, controlled substance analogue, or any other intoxicating substance.

Unlawful Possession of a Firearm by a Convicted Drug Felon; possessing a firearm after having been convicted of a felony drug offense under Title 39, Chapter 17, Part 4, or a felony drug offense in another jurisdiction.
Unlawful Carrying or Possession of a Weapon; carrying a firearm or other deadly weapon with the intent to go armed, outside of statutory lawful-carry exceptions or permitless-carry parameters.

Manufacture, Delivery, or Sale of a Schedule VI Substance; knowingly manufacturing, delivering, selling, or possessing with intent to manufacture, deliver, or sell a substance classified under Schedule VI (e.g., Marijuana) in an amount of not less than 0.5 ounces nor more than 10 pounds.

Simple Possession or Casual Exchange of a Controlled Substance. It criminalizes knowingly possessing or spontaneously exchanging a personal-use quantity of a controlled substance without a valid prescription.

Driving Under the Influence Prohibited. Prohibits driving or exercising physical control of any motor vehicle on public roads or public-frequented premises while under the influence of an intoxicant, marijuana, or drug, or with a BAC/BrAC of 0.08% or higher.
Commercial DUI / Driving Under the Influence (Subsection reference). Cross-referenced under T.C.A. § 55-50-405/408 for BAC limit of 0.04% or greater while operating a commercial vehicle.
Driving Under the Influence. Operating a motor vehicle under the influence of alcohol, drugs, or a combination thereof; or with BAC of 0.08% or higher.
Underage Driving While Impaired. Operating a motorized vehicle by a person aged 16 to 20 with a blood or breath alcohol concentration of 0.02% or higher.

Leaving the Scene of an Accident. Failing to immediately stop a vehicle at the scene of an accident resulting in injury or death, or failing to remain to provide identification and medical assistance.
anchors.subcategory: Leaving the Scene / Hit and Run

anchors.phrase: Reckless Driving. Driving any vehicle in willful or wanton disregard for the safety of persons or property.
Speeding. Operating a motor vehicle in excess of the designated statutory or posted speed limits on state highways or public roadways.
Safety Belt Violation. Operating a passenger motor vehicle on a public highway without wearing a properly adjusted and fastened safety belt.
Driving While License Cancelled, Suspended, or Revoked. Operating a motor vehicle upon a public highway at a time when privileges are actively suspended, cancelled, or revoked by the state.
Illegal Registration or Voting. Intentionally registering to vote or casting a ballot in any election when knowing one is not a qualified, legal, or registered voter in the designated precinct.
Failure to Report Child Abuse. Knowing failure by any mandatory reporter or citizen to report suspected child sexual abuse, physical abuse, or gross neglect.
Abuse or Neglect of an Elderly or Vulnerable Adult. Criminalizes a caregiver's knowing or reckless neglect that causes physical harm or mental anguish.
Violation of an Order of Protection via Contempt. Penalizes individuals who knowingly breach a court-ordered civil or criminal protection decree.
Tax Evasion / Revenue Violations. Covers making willful fraudulent statements, concealing tax assets, or forcibly impeding state revenue officials.
Insurance Fraud (Baseline). Penalizes executing false or misleading claims to obtain insurance payouts or policy advantages.
Grounds for Medical License Discipline. Lists the administrative rules that justify the suspension, restriction, or revocation of a medical license.
Unlawful Hunting Devices or Baiting Wildlife. Penalizes the use of illegal traps, poisons, or artificial baiting structures to harvest game animals.
Unemployment Benefit Misconduct Disqualification. Defines the criteria for denying unemployment benefits to individuals terminated for workplace misconduct or fraud.

Driving Under the Influence (Per Se). Criminalizes operating or being in physical control of any automobile on public roadways while under the influence of any intoxicant, marijuana, controlled substance, or drug that impairs a driver's ability.
Driving Under the Influence (BAC 0.08%+). Establishes a per se violation based entirely on a scientific measure of blood or breath alcohol concentration.
Leaving the Scene of an Accident Involving Injury or Death. Upgrades the offense to a felony if a driver knowingly flees an accident resulting in serious bodily injury or death.
anchors.subcategory: Leaving the Scene / Hit and Run

anchors.phrase: Leaving the Scene of an Accident Involving Death (Enhanced). Establishes heightened felony exposure if the driver knew or should have known the accident resulted in the death of a human being.
anchors.subcategory: Leaving the Scene / Hit and Run

anchors.phrase: Reckless Driving in an Active Work Zone. Penalizes operating a vehicle in willful or wanton disregard for the safety of persons or property within marked highway construction areas.
Driving While License Revoked for DUI. Enhanced penalties if the underlying license revocation was due to a DUI conviction, vehicular assault, or refusal to submit to a chemical test.
Aggravated Abuse or Gross Neglect of an Elderly or Vulnerable Adult. Elevates the offense to a high-grade felony if the knowing abuse results in serious bodily injury or extreme mental trauma.
Major Insurance Fraud. This sub-statute applies theft-grading values to insurance fraud; if the fraudulent claim value is between $10,000 and $60,000, it upgrades the exposure classification.

Adult Driving Under the Influence. This subsection sets the operational baseline prohibiting individuals from operating or sitting in physical control of a motor vehicle with a BAC of 0.08% or higher, or while under the influence of any intoxicant, marijuana, or controlled drug.

Leaving the Scene of an Accident (Property Damage / Simple Injury Baseline). This subsection establishes the duty of a driver involved in an accident resulting in injury or vehicle damage to immediately stop and remain at the scene to provide identification and assistance.
anchors.subcategory: Leaving the Scene / Hit and Run

anchors.phrase: Reckless Driving Baseline. This section establishes the offense of driving any vehicle in willful or wanton disregard for the safety of persons or property.

Driving on a Suspended or Revoked License (Standard Baseline). This subsection criminalizes operating a motor vehicle on a public highway while one's privilege to drive has been cancelled, suspended, or revoked by the state.

Abuse or Neglect of a Vulnerable Adult (Felony Gradation Adjustments). This specific subsection details the penal grading adjustments, classifying reckless neglect as a Class E felony, which scales upward if intentional cruelty or exploitation elements are proven.

Improper Maintenance of Accounts, Deceptive Statements, and False Entries. Intentionally executing or publishing misleading balance books or bank records to obscure financial realities from bank officials or examiners.
Negotiating a Worthless Instrument. Uttering or delivering a check, draft, or order for payment knowing the drawer has insufficient funds or credit with the bank.

Making a false or misleading statement or omitting a material fact during a license application, renewal, or NMLS regulatory registry filing (explicitly failing to disclose disqualifying criminal histories).

Regulatory Disqualification and License Revocation due to a prior felony conviction involving fraud, dishonesty, breach of trust, or money laundering at any point in the applicant's lifespan, or any felony within the preceding 7 years.
Operating an unlicensed banking business, falsely representing oneself as a financial institution with intent to defraud, or engaging in unauthorized fiduciary acts.
Knowingly and willfully engaging in the business of money transmission, wire transfers, or digital currency brokerage without obtaining a state-approved license.

A bank officer or director accepting consumer deposits knowing the financial repository is currently insolvent or in imminent danger of corporate collapse. Also covers embezzlement, abstraction, or willful misapplication of bank moneys by officers, directors, agents, or employees.

A bank officer, director, or employee stipulating, receiving, or agreeing to accept any fee, commission, kickback, or gratuity for procuring or endeavoring to procure a commercial bank loan or line of credit.
Intentionally hiding or concealing any banking transaction, loan, or investment pattern from the bank's directors, auditors, or TDFI investigators.
Knowingly and willfully failing to file mandatory regulatory reports or disclosure sheets required under the state check cashing statutes.
Knowingly and willfully obstructing, delaying, or endeavoring to prevent a lawful regulatory examination of a banking institution or licensee by the state commissioner.

Appropriation of Property, False Entries, or Refusal to Make Reports (Obstruction). Covers bank officer conduct involving misappropriation, falsification, or refusal to cooperate with lawful examination processes.
Operating a shadow banking enterprise or trust company without a valid, issued state or federal banking charter; falsely representing an entity as a licensed bank.
Intentionally hiding loans, discounts, or specific asset transactions from bank directors, auditors, or examining officers.

Unauthorized Money Transmission and Deceptive Regulatory Filings. Operating an unlicensed corporate money transmission network, intentionally omitting required regulatory data, or introducing a material false statement into the state financial registry.

Unlawful Enforcement Conflict of Interest and Receipt of Forbidden Gratuities. Any officer or examiner of the Department of Financial Institutions directly or indirectly receiving payments, personal non-standard loans, or unauthorized equities from an institution they regulate.

False Credit Union Financial Reports and Book Entries. Making, publishing, or altering ledger sheets or balance sheets with intent to deceive credit union members, internal audit boards, or state examiners regarding capital liquidity or asset balances.

Seizure and Forfeiture of Instrumentalities of Unlicensed Money Transmission. Civil confiscation of all property, real or personal, including currency, bank accounts, digital wallets, or servers, utilized or acquired during the operation of an unlicensed money transmitter business.

Unauthorized Practice of Law. Engaging in the practice of law, rendering legal services, or holding oneself out as an attorney in Tennessee without active certification from the Board of Professional Responsibility.

Unlicensed Practice of Medicine. Undertaking to diagnose, treat, operate on, or prescribe for any human disease, pain, injury, or physical condition without holding an active, valid license from the Tennessee Board of Medical Examiners. Each day of continuous unlicensed practice constitutes a separate, distinct felony count.

Unlicensed Selling of Securities or Advising. Transacting commercial corporate securities business or providing professional investment advice within the state of Tennessee without active registration from the Department of Commerce and Insurance.

Unlicensed Real Estate Brokerage and Deceptive Commercial Representation. Acting as a real estate broker, affiliate broker, or timeshare salesperson in Tennessee without holding an active license issued by the Tennessee Real Estate Commission (TREC).

Unlicensed Contracting. Engaging in or offering to engage in commercial or residential contracting where the total cost of the project is $25,000 or more without holding an active, appropriate license.

Fraudulent Insurance Practices and Material Misrepresentation. Intentionally providing misleading, deceptive, or materially untrue data on an insurance application, or misappropriating premiums, or using fraudulent practices in the conduct of business.
Money Laundering Offenses — Knowingly conducting financial transactions using proceeds derived from specified unlawful activities with intent to conceal source, promote crime, or evade reporting.
Child Labor Violations — Employing minors in prohibited occupations, violating maximum school hours, or youth peddling variations.
Failure to Secure Workers' Compensation Insurance — Operating a business without mandatory coverage or intentionally concealing payroll to understate coverage.
Wage Theft / Failure to Pay Wages — Failure to pay employee earned wages within mandatory pay cycles, withholding final paychecks, or executing illegal wage penalties.
anchors.subcategory: Wage Theft / Failure to Pay Wages

anchors.phrase: Military Violations — Conviction via military tribunal for acts equivalent to state-level felonies (theft, espionage, violent crimes).
anchors.subcategory: UCMJ Court-Martial Conviction (Violent Acts / Theft / Espionage)

anchors.phrase: Probation; operational framework for court-ordered community supervision, tracking reporting mandates, curfew controls, drug sweeps, and geofence tracking perimeters.
Probation Revocation; judicial proceeding to revoke a suspended sentence or probation due to technical violations or new criminal conduct.
Parole; conditional supervised release mapping baseline calculation tiers (30%-35% standard offenders vs. up to 100% flat for violent restricted lines).
Parole Revocation; administrative proceeding by the Board of Parole to revoke conditional release for violations.
Community Corrections Placement; residential/intensive community-based alternative sentence instead of a state prison term.
Sentence Reduction Credits; calculation system granting behavior and program performance credits to reduce institutional stay.
Electronic Monitoring; global positioning system tracking required as a mandatory condition of pre-release or community supervision.
Work Release; authorized employment program permitting selected prisoners to leave the facility for outside community labor.
Failure to comply with supervision conditions; technical violation or absconding from probation/parole supervision.
anchors.subcategory: Failure to Comply with Supervision / Absconding

anchors.phrase: Procedural framework governing the trial court's evaluation and revocation of suspended sentences or probation.
Failure to return from work on time under a work release program; legally constitutes prima facie evidence of intent to escape.
anchors.subcategory: Work Release Violation / Failure to Return

anchors.phrase: Trafficking for Commercial Sex Act — Recruiting, enticing, harboring, transporting, or obtaining another person, or benefiting from their provision of a commercial sex act through force, fraud, or coercion.

Human Trafficking (Involuntary Labor Servitude) — Knowingly recruiting, enticing, harboring, transporting, or obtaining another person for forced labor or services through coercion, force, or documentation seizure.
anchors.subcategory: Human Trafficking — Involuntary Servitude / Forced Labor

anchors.phrase: State Military Desertion/Mutiny — Apprehended or ongoing failure to report to duty with intent to remain away permanently, or engaging in subordination while under state active duty orders.
anchors.subcategory: State Military Desertion / Mutiny (Tennessee Code of Military Justice)

anchors.phrase: Money Laundering — knowingly using, attempting, or conspiring to use proceeds derived from specified unlawful activities to conduct financial transactions with intent to conceal their nature, source, location, ownership, or control.
General violations of the Child Labor Act of 1976, including working hours, hazardous duty restrictions, or recordkeeping failures.
Employer failure to secure required workers' compensation insurance or operating without a valid certificate of authority.
Wage Theft — willful failure of an employer to pay employees all agreed-upon, earned wages within statutory bi-monthly or monthly schedule mandates.
anchors.subcategory: Wage Theft / Failure to Pay Wages

anchors.phrase: Hiring, permitting, or suffering an individual to work without first informing the employee of the exact amount of wages to be paid.
anchors.subcategory: Wage Theft / Failure to Pay Wages

anchors.phrase: Federal military court-martial convictions for felony-equivalent acts of violence, theft, or espionage, recognized as prior felonies for sentencing enhancements in state court.
anchors.subcategory: UCMJ Court-Martial Conviction (State Sentencing Enhancement Framework)

anchors.phrase: Desertion, mutiny, or deliberate insubordination by a member of the state military forces while in active state service.
anchors.subcategory: State Military Desertion / Mutiny (Tennessee Code of Military Justice)

anchors.phrase: Aggravated Human Trafficking — recruiting, enticing, harboring, transporting, or obtaining a person for labor or commercial sex through deception, coercion, or force.
anchors.subcategory: Human Trafficking — Involuntary Servitude / Forced Labor

anchors.phrase: Involuntary Labor Servitude — knowingly providing or obtaining the labor or services of a person through force, physical restraint, or a scheme intended to cause the person to believe that failure to perform would result in serious harm.
anchors.subcategory: Human Trafficking — Involuntary Servitude / Forced Labor

anchors.phrase: Trafficking for Commercial Sex Act — knowingly recruiting, enticing, harboring, transporting, or obtaining a person for the purpose of a commercial sex act.
Rape of a Child — Unlawful, non-consensual sexual penetration of a victim under thirteen (13) years of age by an adult.
Sexual Exploitation of a Minor — Knowingly producing, distributing, possessing, or viewing materials/images depicting a minor engaged in explicit sexual conduct.
Threats of Mass Violence Against a School or Public Place — Intentionally making a threat of mass violence against a school or group of people, creating fear or public panic.
Stalking — A willful course of conduct involving repeated harassment or threats that causes a reasonable person to feel terrorized, frightened, intimidated, or threatened.
Sexual Battery — Unlawful, non-consensual intentional sexual contact with a victim accomplished by force, coercion, or where the victim is incapable of giving consent.
Indecent Exposure — Intentionally exposing one's genitals or performing an act of public indecency in a public place or where the conduct is likely to be observed by others.
Unfair or Deceptive Business Practices — Engaging in false advertising, deceptive trade formatting, or fraudulent commercial representation to consumers.
Trust and Restraint of Trade Violations — Coordinated price fixing, asset pooling, or anti-competitive market manipulation to control trade or output within the state.
Mutiny or Sedition — Creating, causing, or conspiring with any other person to create a mutiny, sedition, or collective refusal to obey lawful orders while under active state military authority.
anchors.subcategory: State Military Desertion / Mutiny (Tennessee Code of Military Justice)

anchors.phrase: Desertion — Any member of the state military forces who, without authority, absents themselves from their unit, organization, or place of duty with intent to remain away permanently.
anchors.subcategory: State Military Desertion / Mutiny (Tennessee Code of Military Justice)

anchors.phrase: Absence Without Leave — Any member of the state military forces who, without authority, fails to go to their appointed place of duty at the time prescribed.
anchors.subcategory: State Military Desertion / Mutiny (Tennessee Code of Military Justice)

anchors.phrase: Mutiny — With intent to usurp or override lawful military authority, refusing, in concert with any other person, to obey orders or otherwise comply with duty, or creating any riot or disturbance.
anchors.subcategory: State Military Desertion / Mutiny (Tennessee Code of Military Justice)

anchors.phrase: Trafficking for a Commercial Sex Act — Recruiting, enticing, harboring, transporting, or obtaining another person, or benefiting from a venture, to provide a commercial sex act through force, fraud, or coercion.
Unlicensed Contracting — Engaging in or offering to engage in commercial, industrial, or residential contracting project footprints exceeding $25,000 without holding an active license.

Harassment — Intentionally threatening via telephone, in writing, or electronic communication to take an unlawful action, or placing offensively repetitious/anonymous calls without a legitimate purpose to annoy or alarm.
Stalking — Engaging in a willful course of conduct involving repeated or continuing harassment that would cause a reasonable person to feel terrorized, frightened, intimidated, or threatened.
Rape — Unlawful, non-consensual sexual penetration of a victim accomplished by force, coercion, or where the defendant knows the victim is mentally or physically incapacitated.

Aggravated Rape — Unlawful sexual penetration accompanied by any of the following: use of a deadly weapon, participation of multiple offenders (gang rape), serious bodily injury to the victim, or armed coercion.

Sexual Offense by an Authority Figure — Sexual contact, battery, or penetration of a student or minor victim by an instructional officer, coach, religious leader, or guardian holding explicit position authority.
Coercion of a Witness — Threatening a witness or forcing an individual via deception, coercion, or threat of harm to influence or change their official testimony, or avoid legal service.
Continuous Sexual Abuse of a Child — Engaging in three (3) or more acts of sexual abuse or penetration against one or more minor victims over a continuing period of time.
Violation of the Sexual Offender Registry — Knowingly failing to timely register or report, falsifying address information, or violating residential/work exclusion zone boundaries.
Public Indecency — Knowingly engaging in a public sexual act, public sexual penetration, or public nudity with the intent to arouse or gratify sexual desire.

Civil Rights Intimidation — Intentionally injuring, threatening, or coercing another person with the intent to intimidate them from the free exercise or enjoyment of any constitutional right or privilege.

Extortion — Intentionally using verbal, written, or electronic threats of injury, property damage, or legal accusations to compel another person to perform an act or give up economic assets against their will.

Unlicensed Commercial Operation — Conducting business or offering residential/commercial construction services without state board certification, constituting an automatic per se violation of the Consumer Protection Act.

Probation — A court-ordered suspended execution of a custodial sentence allowing an individual to remain in the community under state or localized supervision, subject to mandatory reporting, random drug sweeps, curfew curtails, and geofence tracking perimeters.

Probation Revocation — A formal court proceeding triggered by a technical or substantive compliance violation, resulting in the termination of a suspended sentence and the activation of custodial confinement.

Parole — Conditional supervised release of an eligible incarcerated individual from physical prison confinement prior to expiration of the sentence, subject to the authority of the Board of Parole (~33% standard vs. 70% violently restricted lines).

Parole Revocation — An administrative or judicial proceeding to revoke parole following a violation of supervision conditions, resulting in the immediate re-confinement of the parolee in a state facility.
Community Correction Center Transfer — A residential or non-residential alternative placement tracking framework mapping local diversion fields as an alternative to or transition from state prison.

Good Time Credits — A statutory sentence reduction mechanism for compliant institutional behavior, program execution, or vocational performance, allowing the advancement of release dates within specified limits.
Electronic Monitoring — Court- or board-ordered GPS or biometric tracking enforced as a non-negotiable condition of pretrial release, probation, or community parole.

Work Release — A supervised employment program allowing incarcerated individuals to leave the local jail facility during specified hours to maintain community employment, subject to mandatory facility return schedules.
Record Sealing — The legal process of restricting public access to eligible historical criminal case files after completion of mandated tracking windows and payment of all court assessments.

Employment Protection Standard — Statutory rules governing employer access and prohibiting discrimination based on closed or expunged entries, allowing candidates to legally deny the existence of the cleared matter during the screening loop.
anchors.subcategory: Effect of Sealed Record on Employment

anchors.phrase: Employer Access Exemptions — The statutory framework outlining specific high-security industries, law enforcement agencies, and care providers that retain explicit lawful authority to bypass sealing orders and review historical entries.
Expungement — The formal judicial process ordering the physical destruction or absolute set-aside of eligible public criminal records, legally transforming the target conviction into a non-event.
DNA Record Expungement — The mandatory process of expunging DNA records, profiles, and biological samples from the state database upon acquittal, dismissal of all charges, or reversal on appeal.
Administrative Record Correction — The official process to correct factual errors, mistaken identity entries, or inaccurate disposition data within central state criminal history databases.

Governor's Pardon — An absolute act of executive clemency issued by the Governor that forgives a criminal conviction and restores core civil rights, but does not physically erase the historical court record unless paired with an expungement order.

Record Sealing (Non-Disclosure Order) — The administrative and judicial filtering of qualifying historical misdemeanor or lower-tier felony records to eliminate general public access while preserving background screening tracking strings for authorized entities.

Employer Access Constraints on Sealed/Expunged Records — Statutory directives outlining which specific employer categories (schools, financial centers, law enforcement) retain historical access to sealed files, and detailing an applicant lawful right to deny the existence of an expunged record.
anchors.subcategory: Effect of Sealed Record on Employment / Employer Access

anchors.phrase: Expungement — The complete, absolute physical destruction of public records, mugshots, booking logs, and arrest data, treating the target transaction or offense as a legal non-event.

DNA Profile Expungement — Mandatory removal and physical destruction of an individual DNA sample and related biological data tracking arrays from the state CODIS central repository database following a clean acquittal, dismissal, or full reversal of all qualifying charges.

Factual Record Correction Challenge — The administrative framework enabling a citizen to formally challenge, audit, and correct historical booking entry errors, misidentified criminal histories, or invalid data entries on their central state RAP sheet.

Executive Pardon — The supreme executive power exercised by the Governor of Tennessee to forgive a criminal conviction, restore full civil liberties, and establish eligibility for post-conviction judicial expungement.

Franchise Rights Restoration Process — The statutory mechanism requiring individuals with felony convictions to fully discharge their sentences, satisfy all court-ordered restitution, stay current on child support, and secure a Certificate of Restoration to restore their voting rights.

Firearm Rights Restoration Process — The statutory and judicial procedure to petition a circuit court for the restoration of firearm possession privileges following the complete discharge of non-disqualifying felony sentences.

Judicial Certificate of Employability/Rehabilitation — A formal court order certifying an offender rehabilitation, designed to relieve occupational licensing bars and insulate employers from negligent hiring liability.
Juvenile Record Sealing — The legal process to seal or expunge juvenile delinquency files, petitions, and orders, restricting access to court administrators and the target individual.

Substantiated Finding of Child Abuse/Maltreatment — A formal administrative determination by the Department of Children Services (DCS), based on a preponderance of the evidence, verifying child abuse, severe neglect, or sexual exploitation.

Substantiated Abuse or Financial Exploitation of an Endangered Adult — A formal administrative or judicial determination verifying physical abuse, severe neglect, or financial exploitation of an elderly or vulnerable adult.

Professional Misconduct Finding — A formal determination by a state licensing board (e.g., Medicine, Nursing, Law, Accountancy) verifying a violation of ethics, gross negligence, or fraudulent practice.
Issuance of Board Final Disciplinary Order — The formal execution and filing of final disciplinary findings and sanctions by a state licensing board following an administrative hearing.

Healthcare Program Exclusion — Mandatory or permissive exclusion from participation in Medicare, Medicaid, and all federal healthcare programs following a conviction for patient abuse, fraud, or licensing board revocations.

Medicaid Exclusion Actions — Administrative finding of provider program fraud, billing abuse, unbundled coding, or kickbacks in the TennCare (Tennessee Medicaid) program, causing formal exclusion from participation as an authorized provider.

MCO Network Exclusion — Formal termination and credentialing exclusion of a provider from managed care organization networks under TennCare for credentialing fraud, falsifying quality metrics, or pattern billing irregularities.

Pharmacy Provider Fraud — Formal finding of prescription drug inventory manipulation, phantom billing, unauthorized generic substitution schemes, or illegal drug diversion tracking profiles within a licensed pharmacy entity.
anchors.subcategory: Pharmacy Provider Fraud and Exclusion Actions

anchors.phrase: Probation — Court-ordered community-based supervision in lieu of active incarceration; tracks mandatory reporting, geographical geofence boundaries, warrantless search waivers, and drug screens.
Probation Revocation — Judicial proceeding triggered by a violation of probation conditions; results in the cancellation of the suspended sentence and execution of the original jail/prison term.
Parole — Conditional supervised release from secure state prison custody prior to sentence expiration; subject to Board of Parole rules and specific calculation tiers.

Parole Revocation — Administrative or judicial adjudication by the Tennessee Board of Parole finding a violation of release conditions, resulting in the cancellation of parole and mandatory re-confinement in a state penitentiary.

Community Corrections Center Transfer — Placement in a highly structured, local residential alternative program or community center instead of state prison confinement, utilizing strict electronic and curfew tracking profiles.
Good Time / Earned Time Credits — Statutory mechanism reducing an individual active prison sentence calculation based on compliance, behavior, and educational milestones within institutional custody.
Electronic Monitoring — Mandatory, continuous global positioning system tracking enforced as a condition of probation, parole, or lifetime public safety supervision for high-risk categories.
