Work Release — Supervised community employment framework allowing specific low-risk incarcerated individuals to depart local jail or workhouse structures for shift work, returning to confinement immediately upon completion.

Record Sealing / Non-Disclosure — Statutory process restricting public exposure to specific historical records after the completion of tracking windows; shifts entries to secure confidential tracks visible only to law enforcement.
Statutory Protection Framework — Rules governing applicant response allowances and corporate hiring constraints following the successful execution of a non-disclosure order.
Employer Access Constraints — Direct statutory framework establishing which high-security corporate sectors and government entities retain lawful access to sealed criminal data histories.

DNA Record Expungement — The mandatory process to purge an individual DNA profile and physical samples from the state CODIS database following an acquittal, a full dismissal of all charges, or a localized reversal on appeal.

Administrative Record Correction — The processing mechanism used to challenge, correct, or delete false, inaccurate, or misattributed entries within the Tennessee Criminal History Database (TBI Repository) via biometric fingerprint comparison reviews.
Governor's Pardon — An absolute act of executive clemency that forgives a criminal conviction and serves as a primary mechanism to restore core civil and political privileges.

Restoration of Voting Rights — The formal procedure requiring completion of all court sentences, parole, and probation, plus full payment of all restitution, court costs, and current child support obligations to secure a Certificate of Restoration.
Order of Restoration of Citizenship — Court-issued decree certifying an individual rehabilitation, full sentence satisfaction, and character compliance to restore lost civil privileges.
anchors.subcategory: Certificate of Rehabilitation / Order of Restoration

anchors.phrase: Administrative Record Correction — The standard agency procedure to correct factual errors, mistaken identities, misapplied felony flags, or missing final dispositions inside the official Tennessee Bureau of Investigation central criminal history databank.

Professional Misconduct Finding — A formal determination by a state professional licensing board (such as the Board of Medical Examiners or Board of Nursing) establishing a violation of statutory ethics, systemic gross negligence, or practice diversion.

Licensing Board Final Order — The formal execution of a final disciplinary decree following a contested administrative panel hearing, establishing permanent practice conditions, license surrenders, or operational bars.

Healthcare Exclusion Registry — Adjudication by the federal Office of Inspector General (OIG), enforced reciprocally by the state, triggering inclusion on the List of Excluded Individuals/Entities (LEIE) for healthcare fraud, patient neglect, or financial abuse.

Violation of Registry Spatial Constraints — Knowingly establishing a living accommodation, primary/secondary residence, or accepting employment within 1,000 feet of a school, daycare, public park, playground, or athletic field.
Abuse of a Corpse — Knowingly and without legal privilege physically mistreating a corpse, disinterring buried human remains, or buying/selling previously interred human skeletal components.

Negligent Release of Confidential Enforcement Data — Any state or local government official who, acting with criminal negligence, leaks or releases information relative to the operational enforcement of federal/state immigration tracking pipelines.
Unlawful Distribution of Hemp Cannabinoids to Minors — Knowingly selling, distributing, or offering products containing hemp-derived cannabinoids to any individual under twenty-one (21) years of age.

Identity Theft — Knowingly obtaining, possessing, buying, selling, or using the personal identifying information of another person without consent to commit any unlawful act or obtain financial benefits.
Parole — Conditional supervised release from incarceration; maps baseline calculation tiers (~30% to 33% standard vs. up to 70% or 100% violently restricted lines).
Parole Revocation — Administrative board proceeding that cancels conditional community release due to technical infractions or new criminal behavior, resulting in a return to prison.
Community Corrections Placement — Residential or intensive non-custodial placement under local community corrections boards as an alternative to or transition from state prison.
Good Time / Earned Time Credits — Statutory sentence reduction mechanism awarded for compliant institutional behavior, educational achievements, or participation in prison work programs.
Electronic Monitoring — Court-ordered or Board-mandated GPS/electronic monitoring as a continuous condition of community supervision or lifetime release.
Work Release — Supervised community employment program allowing incarcerated individuals to leave local jail or workhouse facilities to maintain community employment.
Record Sealing / Non-Disclosure — The statutory procedure restricting general public access to eligible historical criminal histories after the completion of tracking windows.
Employment Rights — Statutory provisions governing employer access to sealed records; grants job applicants the lawful right to deny the existence of a sealed record on applications.
anchors.subcategory: Effect of Sealed Record on Employment

anchors.phrase: Access Limitations — Statutory framework governing which employers retain lawful access to sealed or expunged records (specifically courts, law enforcement, and state licensing agencies).

Expungement — The complete statutory destruction or setting aside of eligible criminal records following dismissals, acquittals, or eligible non-violent historical convictions; treats the target offense as a legal non-event.

DNA Record Expungement — Administrative and physical removal of an individual DNA profile and biological sample from the state central database following an acquittal, a full dismissal, or a non-prosecuted arrest.
Record Correction — Administrative review process designed to correct, modify, or remove factual errors, mistaken identities, or inaccurate disposition logs within state criminal history databases.
Executive Pardon — An act of executive clemency issued by the Governor that forgives a conviction and restores an individual's core civil and political rights.

Criminal Fetal Homicide Statutory Extension — Mandates that for all criminal homicide parts, the terms "another" and "another person" explicitly include a human embryo or fetus at any stage of gestation in utero.
Probation — Court-ordered community supervision following a suspended sentence; mandates strict reporting schedules, curfew curtails, mandatory drug sweeps, and spatial geofence tracking perimeters.
Parole — Conditional supervised release from secure prison custody; governed by baseline calculations ranging from standard 30% or 33% tiers up to 70% or 100% for violently restricted lines.
Parole Revocation — An administrative board proceeding that cancels conditional community release due to technical infractions or new criminal behavior, resulting in a return to prison.
Governor's Pardon — Executive clemency action restoring core civil and citizenship privileges forfeited due to a criminal conviction.
Official Misconduct / Public Contracting Collusion: Entering into an agreement, price-fixing scheme, or kickback arrangement to distort competitive bidding on state, county, or municipal contracts.

Debarment: Formal administrative determination that a contractor is non-responsible due to conviction/civil judgment for fraud, antitrust violations, embezzlement, theft, forgery, or severe contract performance defaults.
Suspension: Immediate temporary exclusion of a contractor from government bidding pending the completion of an investigation, indictment, or legal proceedings.

Non-Procurement Debarment / Limited Denial of Participation: Agency-specific exclusion based on Fair Housing Act violations, equity diversion, systemic food nutrition program fraud, or food safety non-compliance.

Government-Wide Non-Procurement and Procurement Exclusion: The central operational consolidation mechanism that matches, tracks, and applies agency-wide cross-debarment due to systemic corporate fraud or integrity failures.
Aggravated Animal Cruelty; intentionally, knowingly, or maliciously torturing, maiming, burning, or killing a companion animal without a justifiable purpose.
anchors.subcategory: Animal Cruelty / Malicious Animal Abuse or Torture

anchors.phrase: Hunting, Taking, or Pursuing Big Game Out of Season — Unlawful taking of deer, bear, elk, or wild turkey outside of TWRA designated seasons.
anchors.subcategory: Unlawful Hunting / Wildlife Violations — Hunting Big Game Out of Season / Poaching

anchors.phrase: Unlawful Taking, Possession, or Destruction of Endangered or Threatened Species — Exporting, taking, possessing, or shipping wildlife listed on state endangered or threatened species lists.
anchors.subcategory: Unlawful Hunting / Wildlife Violations — Poaching Big Game or Taking Protected State Wildlife

anchors.phrase: Theft of Utility Services — Tampering with meters, pipes, or wires to divert, intercept, or use electricity, gas, or water services without authorization or payment.
Telecommunications Signal Piracy — Intercepting, decoding, or receiving cable, satellite, broadband, or wireless telecommunication transmissions without the provider's authorization.
Unlawful Interception or Network Diversion — Intercepting wire, oral, or electronic communications, or physically diverting public telecommunication infrastructure lines without a lawful warrant.
anchors.subcategory: Diverting or Wiretapping Public Telecom Networks

anchors.phrase: Obstruction of Emergency Communications / Infrastructure Tampering: Intentionally damaging, destroying, disconnecting, or disrupting any 911 line, emergency radio infrastructure, or public safety telecommunication network.
Sexual Activity with an Animal: Engaging in, promoting, or filming sexual conduct or contact with an animal for sexual gratification, commercial, or recreational purposes.
Equine Cruelty / Soring: Unlawfully applying irritating or blistering agents, cuts, or mechanical devices to the limbs of a horse to affect its gait for show or sale.
Assault on a Service Animal or Police K-9: Intentionally killing, injuring, disabling, or interfering with a service animal or law enforcement K-9 performing its duties.

Procurement Fraud / Theft of State Property: Knowingly using false pretenses, inflated invoices, counterfeit materials, or deceptive records to secure public funds from state or municipal purchasing authorities.

False Claims Act Violation: Knowingly presenting or causing to be presented false or fraudulent claims to the state for payment or approval, or using false records to decrease an obligation to the state.

Dogfighting Principal / Promoter: Knowingly owning, possessing, keeping, breeding, training, or selling a dog for the purpose of animal fighting, or manufacturing/possessing gaffs or fighting equipment.
Cockfighting Principal / Promoter: Knowingly housing, breeding, training, selling, or using birds for the purpose of animal fighting, or building/maintaining a cockfighting pit.
Animal Fighting Spectator: Knowingly being present as a spectator at any place, building, or structure where preparations are being made or an exhibition of animal fighting is occurring.

Theft of Services / Mid-Tier Utility Tampering: Unlawfully bypassing, altering, or diverting electrical, gas, or water utility services where the total retail value of the stolen service or infrastructure damage is between $2,500 and $9,999.

Theft of Services / High-Tier Utility Tampering: Unlawfully bypassing, altering, or diverting electrical, gas, or water utility services where the total retail value of the stolen service or infrastructure damage is between $10,000 and $59,999.

Direct Interest Conflict in Public Contracting: A public officer or corporate partner knowingly maintaining a direct personal or corporate financial equity stake in a public contract awarded by their own municipal, county, or state agency.

Indirect Interest Conflict in Public Contracting: Failing to publicly disclose an indirect financial interest (such as a spouse's employment or distant corporate entity) in a public contract or failing to completely abstain from voting on that contract.
Statutory grounds for disciplinary actions administered by the Board of Examiners in Psychology.
Grounds for denial, withholding, restricting, or permanently revoking counseling licensure.
Engaging in unauthorized practice, advertising psychological capabilities while unlicensed, or violating testing technician protocols.
Rules governing the systematic administration of civil penalties and licensing discipline for psychological practices.
Breach of the standardized ethical principles governing health service providers, including sexual boundary violations, dual relationships, or exploitation of patients.
Making false claims, altering documents, procuring a license by fraud/misrepresentation, or committing insurance fraud/billing fraud in active professional practice.
Personal addiction or abuse of alcohol, controlled substances, or stimulants that severely impairs a practitioner's fitness for duty.
Active violation or attempted violation of statutory chapter mandates or regulations.
Gross healthcare liability or systematic negligence within active psychological operations.
Possession of an active felony conviction under any state or federal jurisdiction as automatic grounds for license denial or termination.

Violating board regulations, with a specific statutory exemption that a violation of section A.11.b of the 2014 ACA Code of Ethics (value-based client referrals) does not constitute disciplinary grounds unless the client was in imminent danger of self-harm or harm to others.
Delivering psychological services to the public, charging fees, or utilizing restricted legal titles ("Psychologist", "Psychological Examiner") without holding an active state license.
Unlawful administration, evaluation, scoring, or interpretive reporting of restricted psychological, personality, or neuropsychological test batteries by uncertified staff.
Type A civil penalty violations involving imminent threats to client health/safety.
Type B civil penalty violations indicating high probability of serious patient harm.
Type C administrative infractions including address update failures or minor continuing education non-compliance.
Formal administrative board disciplinary mechanisms including public reprimand, probation, suspension, or permanent revocation of psychology licensure.
Mandatory assessment of state investigative, legal, and hearing costs against a disciplined licensee following a contested case.
The authorization, structure, and operational deployment of screening panels to review and determine probable cause for provider misconduct.
Failure of a healthcare practitioner (including psychologists and counselors) to update or provide required profiles, criminal histories, or malpractice histories to the Department of Health.
Violating cross-border telepsychology rules or temporary in-person practice requirements authorized under the Psychology Interjurisdictional Compact (PSYPACT).
Out-of-state regulatory violations, billing anomalies, or clinical malpractice while utilizing the privilege to practice under the multi-state counseling compact framework.

The mandatory reciprocal enforcement requirement forcing separate boards to take disciplinary action if a dually licensed clinician (e.g., licensed counselor and licensed social worker/marital therapist) is disciplined by another board for fraud or misconduct.

The willful failure of a mandated reporter—specifically including licensed psychologists, therapists, and counselors—to report known or suspected abuse, neglect, or financial exploitation of an institutionalized or vulnerable adult.

Knowing or intentional failure of any person (including a licensed clinician) to immediately report suspected child abuse, severe abuse, or neglect to the Department of Children's Services (DCS) or local law enforcement.
Receiving a conviction for a felony, or a misdemeanor involving moral turpitude, in any state or federal court jurisdiction.
Practicing psychology without a license under aggravated circumstances, such as causing serious bodily/psychological injury or maintaining persistent identity fraud.
Cruelty to Animals: Unlawfully failing to provide adequate food, water, care, or shelter, or abandoning an animal in one's custody.

Unauthorized Wiretapping or Network Interception: Intentionally intercepting, wiretapping, or obstructing public telecommunications networks or electronic communications without a lawful judicial warrant or authorization.
Knowing failure of a mandatory reporter to report suspected child abuse or neglect.
A second or subsequent violation of the mandatory duty to report child abuse or neglect.
Intentional failure to report suspected child abuse or neglect, reflecting deliberate cover-up or concealment.
Procedural mechanisms giving the juvenile court jurisdiction to hear, fine, or bind over mandatory reporters to criminal grand juries.
Intentionally or knowingly making a false report of child abuse, severe abuse, or neglect to DCS or law enforcement.
A second or subsequent violation of intentionally making a false report of child abuse or neglect, or reports engineered to intentionally disrupt a family or business structure.
Failure of any person, specifically a licensed clinician, to report a known or suspected situation of child sexual abuse.
Intentionally or knowingly filing a false report of child sexual abuse, generating catastrophic legal duress for the falsely accused.
Knowingly operating a major stationary source of air pollution without an active Title V operating permit or in direct violation of emissions standards.
Knowingly making false representations in air emissions monitoring logs or intentionally withholding required Title V program fees.
Knowingly storing, treating, transporting, or disposing of hazardous waste substances without a valid manifest or permit.
Engaging in, bidding on, or performing asbestos abatement, removal, or encapsulation projects without holding a valid, active state asbestos contractor license or certification.
Knowingly failing to report a detected petroleum or chemical release from an underground storage tank system, or intentional disabling of tank leak detection systems.
Operating an unregistered, untagged underground storage tank system or delivery of petroleum products into a system tagged with an active TDEC delivery prohibition red tag.

Willfully falsifying, manipulating, or manufacturing fraudulent soil, water, or environmental remediation reports within the state Voluntary Cleanup Program (VCP) or Brownfields revitalization structures to secure public funds or commercial liability waivers.

Operating a commercial solid waste hauling service without active TDEC registration, or illegal dumping/disposal of solid waste on public or private property not permitted as a solid waste disposal facility.
Commercial dumping of large-scale solid waste volumes (exceeding 500 pounds or 100 cubic feet) for commercial profit on unpermitted lands or waterways.
Discharging unpermitted sewage, industrial waste, or other pollutants into waters of the state without an active National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) permit.
Administrative determination of non-compliance resulting in the assessment of civil financial penalties and cost liability for damages to the state's natural resources.
Knowingly operating, delivering petroleum product into, or maintaining an underground storage tank system without a valid compliance certificate from the state.
Intentionally falsifying engineering soil data, water sample records, or remediation reports submitted under the Tennessee Voluntary Cleanup and Property Redevelopment Act.
Operating a solid waste processing or disposal facility/hauling operation without a valid permit, or illegal dumping of commercial solid waste on unapproved public/private land.
Performing asbestos abatement, encapsulation, or removal operations in public or commercial facilities without holding an active, unrevoked state asbestos contractor accreditation.
Manufacturing, processing, packing, or holding food under unsanitary conditions whereby it may have become contaminated with filth or rendered injurious to public health.
Knowingly selling, giving, or furnishing alcoholic beverages to any person under twenty-one (21) years of age.
Knowingly selling, furnishing, giving, or causing to be sold, furnished, or given, any alcoholic beverage, wine, or beer to any person under twenty-one (21) years of age.

Operating a retail food establishment, commercial kitchen, mobile food unit, or catering facility without securing or maintaining an active public health permit from the state or local county health department.

Operating a substance abuse treatment facility, non-residential opioid treatment program (OTP), detoxification unit, or sober living home without holding an active facility license from the Department of Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services.

Operating a residential or community-based substance abuse treatment facility, methadone clinic, or Office-Based Opiate Treatment (OBOT) program without a valid license from the Department of Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services (DMHSAS).

Knowingly manufacturing, delivering, selling, or possessing with intent to manufacture, deliver, or sell a controlled substance classified under Schedules I through VII of the Tennessee Controlled Substances Act.

Acquiring or obtaining possession of a controlled substance by misrepresentation, fraud, forgery, deception, subterfuge, or the use of a fraudulent DEA registration or prescription pad (commonly including clinician "doctor shopping" or prescription diversion).

Dispensing, distributing, prescribing, or administering a controlled substance by a practitioner or entity without holding a valid, active state license, pharmacy registration, or specialized DEA certificate.
Manufacturing, processing, packing, holding, or selling food products that are adulterated, decomposed, contaminated with filth, or prepared under unsanitary conditions endangering public health.
Knowingly presenting, or causing to be presented, a false or fraudulent claim for payment or approval under the Medicaid/TennCare program.
Knowingly and willfully making or causing to be made any false statement or representation of a material fact in any application for TennCare benefits or provider payments.

Statutory mandate requiring Tennessee health boards to enact suspension or revocation against any practitioner who has been excluded, suspended, or terminated from an out-of-state Medicaid program (e.g., an Arkansas Medicaid Exclusion).

State statutory alignment mechanism requiring immediate termination of employment or billing capability for any individual found on the Federal OIG List of Excluded Individuals/Entities (LEIE) for healthcare fraud or patient neglect.
Statutory mandate compelling all state licensing boards and medical malpractice insurers to report any adverse licensing actions, malpractice payments, or clinical privilege restrictions to the NPDB.
Soliciting, receiving, offering, or paying any kickback, bribe, or rebate for referring an individual for TennCare/Medicaid covered services.
Conspiring with one or more persons to get a false or fraudulent claim allowed or paid under the TennCare/Medicaid program.
Preparing or presenting false, incomplete, or misleading material statements in support of a claim for payment or benefit under an insurance policy.
Administrative regulation empowering the Bureau of TennCare to execute summary suspension of billing lines and immediate recoupment of funds based on a credible allegation of provider fraud.
Knowingly failing to install or maintain mandated fire suppression systems, alarms, or emergency egress structures in high-occupancy commercial properties.
Acting as a residential roofing services provider without proper state registration, or violating statutory rules regarding insurance deductible offsets and consumer contract parameters.
Operating any elevator, moving walk, or escalator for public use without an active certificate of operation issued by the Department of Labor and Workforce Development.
Operating high-pressure boilers or chemical pressure vessels without an active state safety inspection validation certificate or defying emergency repair notices.
Retaining or misapplying construction loan draws, multi-family down payments, or commercial project funds intended for sub-contractors, laborers, or material suppliers.
Operating a commercial enterprise installing fire, burglar, or safety tracking alarm systems, or providing security services without an active specialty company license from the state.

Administrative code framework establishing the direct assessment matrix for Type A and Type B structural contractor safety failures, unpermitted activities, or aiding and abetting unlicensed operations.

Fraud or deceit in obtaining a certificate, gross negligence, dishonesty, or incompetence in the practice of public accountancy, or failing to maintain professional standards during corporate financial audits.

Gross negligence in patient care delivery, practicing under the influence of intoxicating substances, or the intentional diversion of controlled substances from an ambulance or clinical storage center by an EMT, Advanced EMT, or Paramedic.

Dismissal or suspension of licensed educators based on incompetence, inefficiency, neglect of duty, unprofessional conduct, felony convictions, or official Department of Children's Services (DCS) substantiated findings of child maltreatment or physical/emotional abuse.
Knowingly failing to pay accrued employee wages, misapplying or unlawfully retaining employee tip pools, or intentionally making shortfalls below statutory wage structures.
Operating a nursing home or assisted living center in persistent violation of statutory safety, hygiene, or clinical monitoring levels, leading to active resident endangerment.
Engaging in unprofessional, dishonorable, or unethical conduct, or demonstrating gross malpractice or persistent negligence in medical practice.

Obtaining or attempting to procure a nursing license through fraud, deceit, or material misrepresentation, or engaging in narcotics diversion or active chemical impairment during clinical shift tracking.
Violating state pharmacy compounding standards, dispensing toxic alterations, or failing to maintain drug safety tracking protocols under the Board of Pharmacy.

Intentionally misclassifying workforce members as independent contractors, making false statements regarding payroll records, or hiding corporate payroll structures to evade mandatory state unemployment fund payments.
Administrative code protocol defining the specific algorithmic application of penalty enhancements for repeat corporate structural labor safety, classification failures, or wage tracking failures.
Making any substantial and willful misrepresentation regarding real property or transaction terms to induce a party to execute a contract.
Failing to timely deposit, maintain, or account for trust funds, earnest money, or escrow deposits, or commingling client funds with personal/corporate operating accounts.

Developing an appraisal report containing fraud, deceit, or gross negligence, or violating Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP) guidelines to artificially alter real property valuation calculations.
Intentional conversion or misappropriation of client funds held within an Interest on Lawyers' Trust Accounts (IOLTA) infrastructure, or systematic fraud causing severe client loss.
Knowingly or willfully performing any official notary act through fraud, certifying a signature without the signing party physically present, or executing a false certificate.
Operating an unpermitted shop, practicing cosmetology, barbering, or aesthetics without an active state license, or maintaining severe, repeated health/sanitation violations.
Charging extortionate premium rates, soliciting business inside local jail facilities, failing to maintain statutory capital reserves, or employing convicted felons as runners.
Making willful or substantial misrepresentations, failing to timely account for or remit escrow funds, practicing undisclosed dual agency, or accepting commissions outside the principal broker.
Engaging in attorney professional misconduct, including co-mingling or stealing client trust funds (IOLTA fraud), severe neglect of client legal matters, or felony criminal tracking.
Executing false certificates, notarizing legal documents or real estate deeds without physical or certified online presence, or failing to maintain required digital security journal parameters.

Engaging in false or misleading advertising, using 'shills' or fake bidders to artificially drive up property prices, or failing to properly maintain, separate, and timely account for escrow or consignment sales funds belonging to others.
Unlawfully soliciting bail bond business inside a local jail facility, courthouse, or police precinct, or paying kickbacks to jailers or police personnel for client referrals.
Failing to maintain statutory capital reserves, failing to pay final judgments on forfeited bonds, or misapplying consumer collateral assets held in trust.
Submitting fraudulent or intentionally fabricated investigation reports to clients, executing illegal wiretapping, or engaging in unpermitted GPS tracking.
Knowingly notarizing a forged signature, executing a false certificate, or certifying an acknowledgment without the signing party physically present before the officer.
Intentionally altering or falsifying an official judicial deposition or trial transcript, or practicing court reporting without holding an active state license.

Utilizing fraudulent or deceptive collection mechanisms, violating the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA) parameters systematically, or commingling client recovery pools with corporate operating reserves.
Operating an unpermitted cosmetology, barber, or aesthetics shop, or demonstrating repeated, severe health and sanitation infractions creating bloodborne pathogen exposure risks to the public.
Acts or omissions violating the Rules of Professional Conduct, conviction of a serious crime, or willful refusal to comply with a court order.

Misrepresentation or fraud in the conduct of a funeral establishment, false or misleading advertising, solicitation of dead human bodies, or utilizing previously used caskets without new interior inserts.

Knowingly purchasing scrap metal products or catalytic converters without verifying identification, failing to maintain transactional tracking databases, or paying in cash to evade state verification laws.
Utilizing unapproved psychological testing baseline tracking protocols, certifying fabricated analysis metrics, or operating as a polygraph practitioner without an active, valid state license.

Operating a mobile or commercial locksmith enterprise without active state registration, duplicating high-security keys without verifying identification or maintaining ownership logs, or providing bypassing entry assets to unauthorized persons.

Certifying an altered, false, or misleading judicial transcript, intentionally omitting cross-examination testimony line items, or practicing verbatim or stenographic reporting without active state board credentials.

Defrauding any buyer or selling party, making intentional misrepresentations regarding vehicle structural history, or engaging in illegal odometer rolling/tampering to inflate commercial asset metrics.

Executing predatory or unlawful non-consent commercial towing actions on private property, refusing vehicle retrieval access to legitimate owners, or enforcing price-gouging structures exceeding municipal statutory fee caps.
Operating a commercial household goods moving infrastructure system or intrastate transport cargo enterprise without holding an active certificate of public convenience and necessity from the state.
Accepting, giving or charging any undisclosed rebate, kickback, or unearned profit on expenditures made for a principal in a real estate transaction.
Procuring or attempting to procure an appraiser registration, license, or certification by making false statements, falsifying logs, or hiding disqualifying metrics.

Engaging in any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud clients, executing unauthorized trades ("churning"), misapplying client capital portfolios, or practicing as an investment adviser or broker-dealer agent without active registration.

Misappropriating or converting any monies or properties received in the course of doing insurance business, intentionally forging an applicant's signature, or selling insurance without an active license.

Engaging in any practice to defraud borrowers or lenders, fabricating consumer asset verification data, equity skimming, or practicing as a mortgage loan originator or broker without an active license.
Operating a commercial beauty salon, barber shop, aesthetics parlor, or nail boutique facility without obtaining a valid, active shop license from the state.
Utilizing false, deceptive, or highly misleading bait-and-switch advertising models to procure automotive consumers, or intentionally hiding mandatory fee additions.

Intentionally withholding a towed vehicle from its legitimate owner, refusing to permit the owner to retrieve life-essential personal property (e.g., medical devices, child seats), or demanding cash-only payment methods under extortionate threats.
Acting for more than one (1) party in a transaction without the knowledge and written consent of all parties thereto (Undisclosed Dual Agency).
Failure to maintain client funds in an identifiable IOLTA or trust account, commingling personal funds with client funds, or misappropriation/defalcation of client trust property.
Stamping or sealing architectural plans without an active registration, or allowing an unlicensed party to use an official professional seal.
Performing athletic training services, injury assessments, or therapeutic treatment protocols outside the direct referral or operational direction of a licensed physician.
Staging professional boxing, mixed martial arts (MMA), or combat sports events without an active promoter license, or intentionally concealing a fighter's failed medical/drug screening metrics.
Committing fraud, deceit, or gross negligence in compiling geological field maps, or intentionally falsifying subsurface shale, silt, or groundwater soil reports.
Prescribing or administering therapeutic pharmaceutical agents or ophthalmic medications without holding an active therapeutic certification endorsement from the Board.

Employing 'cappers' or 'steerers' to solicit accident victims, executing predatory spinal-screening solicitation schemes at public venues via deceptive medical claims, or over-utilizing chiropractic services for insurance maximization.
Diverting animal controlled substances (such as ketamine, opioids, or barbiturates) for human use, or failing to maintain strict statutory log boundaries under the Veterinary Practice Act.
Exhibiting gross malpractice, systematic surgical incompetence, or executing unpermitted foot/ankle procedures extending past statutory medical boundary definitions.
Practicing or offering to practice speech pathology or audiology without a valid state license, or engaging in exploitative billing protocols for non-rendered rehabilitative services.
Operating a commercial private investigation company or executing unpermitted surveillance and data background screening actions without an active license from the state.
Intentionally falsifying engineering soil data, water table samples, structural shale layout evaluations, or public environmental impact statements.

Prescribing or administering therapeutic pharmaceutical agents without specialized board certification, or prescribing controlled substances outside the explicit scope of eye care treatment protocols.

Utilizing predatory, deceptive, or prohibited solicitation methods (such as direct steering loops at accident scenes or employing 'runners' to procure accident victims) to artificially drive up health insurance billings.

Diverting Schedule II, III, or IV controlled substances from a veterinary facility for unauthorized human consumption, prescribing phantom animal charts to procure narcotics, or severe animal cruelty/neglect convictions.
Demonstrating gross healthcare liability, structural surgical negligence, or persistent clinical incompetence in lower extremity surgeries or complex podiatric wound care interventions.

Practicing or offering to practice speech-language pathology or audiology services without holding an active, valid state license, or utilizing fraudulent credentials to secure school system therapy billing.
Utilizing fraudulent diagnostic parameters to sell unneeded medical hearing hardware, bait-and-switch advertising patterns targeting elder consumers, or practicing without a valid license.

Intentionally fabricating or falsifying soil percolation data, absorption profiles, or safety tracking logs submitted to municipal or state entities for septic tank capability and environmental structural approvals.

Failing to complete the mandated forty (40) hours of continuing education during a two-year licensing cycle, or fabricating attendance/completion certificates for ethics or cultural diversity requirements.
Publishing false, deceptive, or highly misleading advertisements regarding dental specialty certifications, hiding mandatory dynamic add-on fees, or promising guaranteed clinical outcomes.

Engaging in sexual relationships, dual boundary exploitation, or emotional/financial victimization with a current client or a former client within the contractually barred multi-year post-termination window.

Procedural mechanism to appeal an emergency summary license suspension enacted by a Department of Health Board upon a finding that public health, safety, or welfare imperatively requires emergency action.
Statutory mechanism allowing an aggrieved licensee to bypass long-term administrative delays and appeal a board's final or emergency summary suspension order directly to a court of law.

The specific statutory sub-provision governing the application for an emergency interim stay of an agency decision, requiring the petitioner to clear four traditional equitable injunctive hurdles in open court.

The specific statutory sub-provision dictating the narrow grounds upon which a court may reverse or modify an agency action, strictly prohibiting Chancellors from substituting their judgment on factual determinations.

Health Care Consumer Right-to-Know Act: Failure of a licensed healthcare practitioner to report any medical malpractice judgment, award, or settlement exceeding $25,000 to their respective state licensing board within 30 days.

Grounds for Discipline of Osteopathic Physicians: Failure to cross-report clinical privilege restrictions, peer review discipline, or professional liability payments to the Board of Osteopathic Examination to ensure alignment with federal NPDB reporting rules.

Board of Dentistry - Duty to Report Convictions: Failure of a licensed dental professional to report any felony conviction or fraud-related misdemeanor conviction to the board within 10 days of the entry of judgment.

Reporting of Medical Malpractice Claims Data: Failure of professional liability insurance entities or self-insured health risk systems to report mandatory annual claims history, payment resolutions, and individual practitioner data to the department.
Tennessee False Claims Act: Knowingly presenting, or causing to be presented, a false or fraudulent claim for payment or approval involving state-funded healthcare programs or Medicaid allocations.

Child Abuse Reporting Mandate: Establishes a universal legal requirement that any person who has knowledge or reasonable cause to suspect that a child is a victim of abuse, neglect, or sexual exploitation must report it immediately to the Department of Children's Services or law enforcement.
Failure of a licensed healthcare practitioner to report medical malpractice judgments, awards, or settlements exceeding $25,000 to their respective state licensing board within 30 days.
Regulatory mandate requiring licensees to report any felony conviction or specific fraud/theft misdemeanor to the board within 30 days of the conviction date.
Failure by an insurance company, risk retention group, or self-insured entity to report complete malpractice claim and resolution data annually to the state.
Establishes the universal legal obligation for every person in Tennessee to report known or reasonably suspected cases of child abuse or neglect immediately.

TennCare Fraud — Knowingly obtaining, attempting to obtain, or aiding another to obtain medical assistance or provider payments via fraudulent statements, misrepresentations, or willful concealment of material facts.

Failure to Report Child Abuse — A second or subsequent violation of failing to report child abuse, or any single instance where the person intentionally fails to make the required report of child abuse.
Minor Administrative Violation — Any technical, facility, or paperwork standard violation that is not explicitly designated as a major violation or a safety risk under department protocols.
Stacking Major Violation — Having six (6) or more minor violations of any type within a 3-month block, or three (3) or more minor violations of the exact same regulation within a 3-month block.

Failure to Report Clinical Privilege Restrictions — Failure by a chief medical officer, medical entity, or peer review committee to report any restriction, suspension, or surrender of a physician's clinical privileges lasting over 30 days due to competence or professional misconduct.

Failure to Report Malpractice Claim Data — Failure of an insurance entity, risk retention group, or self-insured healthcare provider to electronically transmit comprehensive malpractice claim files, dispositions, and settlement values to the state database.

Violation of Affirmative Reporting Mandate — Every person having reasonable cause to suspect that a child has been subjected to child abuse or neglect must report that knowledge or suspicion immediately. This specific section creates the core universal mandatory legal duty.

Failure to Report Child Abuse (Baseline Misdemeanor) — Knowingly failing to provide immediate notification to the Department of Children's Services or local law enforcement when there is a reasonable suspicion of child abuse or neglect.
Malicious False Reporting — Knowingly making a false report of child abuse, sexual abuse, or neglect with malice, intent to defame, or intent to manipulate legal or domestic proceedings.

Intentionally Withholding or Falsifying Information — Intentionally making false statements, misrepresenting staff credentials, or withholding material safety information during child care license applications or state inspections.

Inspection Obstruction & Safety Plan Violation — Blocking, delaying, or refusing entry to DHS inspectors, or willfully failing to execute an active emergency child-safety plan ordered by the department.

Negligent Injury Violation — Any violation of agency safety, staffing, or structural standards that directly results in injury, medical emergency, or physical harm to a child under the facility's supervision.
Tennessee Identity Theft Deterrence and Credit Protection Act. Unlawful deployment, misuse, or failure to secure consumer credit reports, security freezes, and personal identifying information (PII).
Statutory cross-activation of a Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA) bar or suspension for failing to deliver information, books, or records requested under FINRA Rule 8210.
Engaging in acts, practices, or courses of business by an investment adviser that operate as fraud, deceit, or an explicit breach of the core fiduciary duty to a client.

Adoption of an active Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) administrative sanction order finding serious, systemic violations of federal securities rules (e.g., Securities Act of 1933, Investment Advisers Act of 1940).
Serious violations of the Rules of Professional Conduct (including conversion of client trust funds, perjury, or systemic unauthorized practice of law).
Residential or commercial contracting violations, including operating beyond license monetary ceilings, safety infractions, or failure to secure and maintain a valid surety bond.
Insurance Producer Fraud and Misrepresentation; committing an unfair trade practice, fraud, or intentional material misrepresentation in the solicitation or sale of an insurance policy.

Unlicensed Real Estate Brokerage / Broker Misrepresentation; acting as a real estate broker, affiliate broker, or timeshare salesperson without a valid license, or engaging in flagrant misrepresentation while licensed.

Uncertified Corporate Trade Practice / Corporate Impersonation of Regulated Fields; operating an entity or holding out an entity to practice law, medicine, securities sales, or regulated commercial contracting without state board certification.

Failure to Provide Information / FINRA Non-Compliance Bar; failing to provide testimony, documents, or written responses requested by FINRA during an investigation, triggering an automatic cross-jurisdictional state-level revocation.

SEC Administrative Cease-and-Desist Enforcement; being subject to an order of the Securities and Exchange Commission denying, suspending, or revoking registration, creating an automatic basis for state disciplinary enforcement.
Unauthorized Practice of Medicine; practicing medicine, surgery, or obstetrics without a license from the Board of Medical Examiners, or holding oneself out as a licensed physician.

Illegal Corporate Practice of Medicine; a non-physician owned business entity or corporate structure employing physicians to practice medicine or controlling clinical decision-making without satisfying statutory exemptions (e.g., licensed hospitals or designated clinics).
Commercial Fee-Splitting and Medical Rebating; a physician dividing fees, offering rebates, or paying kickbacks to an unlicensed individual or corporate entity in exchange for patient referrals.
Unlicensed Pharmacy Operation or Drug Wholesaling; operating a pharmacy, outsourcing facility, or wholesale drug distribution facility without an active license from the Tennessee Board of Pharmacy.
Wholesale Drug Diversion and Peddling; knowingly purchasing, selling, or transferring prescription drugs via unauthorized pedigree layouts or from uncertified secondary wholesalers.

Unlicensed Chiropractic Practice / Scope Exceedance; practicing chiropractic adjustments, spinal manipulation, or holding oneself out as a chiropractor without an active license from the Board of Chiropractic Examiners.

Unauthorized Practice of Professional or Practical Nursing; practicing as an RN, LPN, or Advanced Practice Registered Nurse (APRN), or utilizing matching credentials without an active license from the Tennessee Board of Nursing.

Boating Under the Influence (BUI) First Offense; operating any vessel, motorboat, personal watercraft (PWC), or sailboat on any public or state waters while under the influence of an intoxicant, marijuana, narcotic drug, or substance affecting the central nervous system, or with a blood/breath alcohol concentration (BAC) of 0.08% or greater.

Boating Implied Consent Violation; refusing to submit to a chemical test (blood, breath, or urine) for the purpose of determining alcohol or drug content when requested by a law enforcement officer or TWRA agent with reasonable cause.

Aggravated Boating Under the Influence / Vehicular Assault by Vessel; operating a watercraft while intoxicated and proximately causing serious bodily injury to another person during a collision, wreck, or erratic navigation maneuver.

Boating Under the Influence (BUI) Second Offense; operating a vessel or watercraft on state or public waters while intoxicated, having one prior BUI or DUI conviction within a 10-year lookback period.

Boating Under the Influence (BUI) Third or Subsequent Offense; operating any vessel or watercraft while under the influence of alcohol, narcotics, or central nervous system depressants, with two or more prior BUI or DUI convictions within a 10-year lookback window.
Boating Under the Influence with a Minor Passenger; operating any vessel or watercraft while intoxicated on public or state waters while accompanied by a passenger under the age of eighteen (18).

Vehicular Homicide by Vessel (BUI Felony Homicide); the reckless killing of another person as the proximate result of operating a vessel, watercraft, or motorboat while under the influence of an intoxicant, drug, or marijuana.

Aggravated Boating Under the Influence; operating any vessel, motorboat, or watercraft in a reckless, negligent, or erratic manner while under the influence of an intoxicant, and directly causing a multi-vessel collision, structural disaster, or serious bodily injury to another individual.

Operating Unregistered Commercial Watercraft; operating, or giving permission for the operation of, any commercial passenger-carrying vessel, charter vessel, or commercial shipping watercraft on state or public waters without an active Certificate of Number or valid registration.

Commercial Fleet Safety Failure / Passenger-for-Hire Equipment Violation; failure of a commercial watercraft fleet operator, passenger boat, or livery operator to maintain state-mandated life-saving equipment, structural hulls, fire suppression devices, or U.S. Coast Guard-approved safety modifications.

Knowing Authorization of Illegal Vessel Operation; an owner or person directly in charge of a watercraft knowingly authorizing or permitting the vessel to be operated on state waters in direct violation of state registration, safety, or licensing mandates.

Altering or Forging State Lottery Tickets; knowingly influencing, altering, counterfeiting, or forging a lottery ticket issued under the Tennessee Education Lottery Corporation framework for the purpose of deceptive entry or financial gain.

Uttering or Presenting a Forged Lottery Ticket; knowingly presenting, uttering, or attempting to redeem an altered, counterfeited, or forged lottery ticket or prize claim to the lottery corporation, a retail agent, or state authority for monetary payout.

Insider Lottery Manipulation / Employee Lottery Fraud; an officer, employee, contractor, or retail operator of the Tennessee Education Lottery Corporation knowingly rigging data feeds, pre-determining winning draw patterns, or falsifying corporate operational metrics for private profit.

Lottery Fiduciary Defalcation and Conversion; failure of a certified lottery retailer or its corporate officers to preserve, account for, and remit ticket sales proceeds, treating public ticket revenues as a personal or corporate slush fund.

Criminal Simulation of Gaming Security Markers; making or altering an object (such as scratch-off ticket bar codes, security foils, or thermal print markers) so that it appears to have an authenticity, rarity, or source value that it does not possess.

Forgery of Financial Instruments; forging, executing, or uttering any written state instrument or prize assignment document with the intent to defraud, where the value of the target instrument dictates the final felony grade matching standard theft ladders.

Critical Infrastructure Interference (Port Facility); knowingly interrupting, interfering with, destroying, or injuring the operations or infrastructure of railroads, commercial maritime shipping channels, river ports, loading docks, or public transport terminals.

Breach of Port Authority Directives; violating lawful safety orders, berthing constraints, hazardous cargo positioning layouts, or maritime safety rules established by a regional or municipal Tennessee Port Authority.

Restricted Maritime Security Boundary Breach; entering or remaining within a designated maritime secure zone, deepwater commercial harbor perimeter, or safety zone established under the Maritime Transportation Security Act (MTSA) without authorization.

Public Property and Port Obstruction; establishing unapproved structures, blocking, or unauthorized camping/occupying on public property, state loading docks, or river port roadways, effectively obstructing transit access lines to regulated shipping infrastructure.

Violation of Regional Port Authority Structural Mandate; establishing unapproved docks, altering channels, or erecting marine obstructions within the geographic boundary of a certified Tennessee Port Authority without active engineering permits.

Criminal Trespass on Restricted Infrastructure; entering or remaining on any restricted, fenced, or clearly posted maritime facility, river port loading deck, or harbor storage terminal without the explicit consent of the property owner or port supervisor.

Failure to Confine a Dog / Animal At Large; allowing a dog or animal under one's control to go uncontrolled upon the premises of another without consent, or upon a highway, public road, street, or any other place open to the public generally.

Failure to Confine a Vicious Dog Resulting in Injury; an owner or keeper failing to maintain containment of a known vicious or dangerous stray animal, directly resulting in the animal attacking and causing bodily injury to a human being off the owner's property.

Aggravated Cruelty to Animals; knowingly and brutally torturing, crushing, starving, or maiming a captive dangerous stray, feral animal, or companion animal, or unreasonably withholding sustenance resulting in serious physical injury or death.

Defiance of Stray Animal Rabies Quarantine; harboring or concealing a stray or feral animal that has bitten a human being, or refusing to surrender the animal to public health authorities or animal control for a mandatory 10-day clinical observation and quarantine period.

Refusal to Remediate Public Health Hazards; failure or explicit refusal of a real property owner or custodian to clear, clean, or remediate a parcel or building officially designated as a chronic public health hazard, environmental menace, or vermin harboring point following statutory county notice.

Uniform Building Code Non-Compliance; constructing, altering, or maintaining a commercial, multi-family, or residential building in direct defiance of safety regulations established by the State Fire Marshal, creating life-safety defects, structural fire traps, or unsafe exit-way configurations.

Vandalism of Public Infrastructure / State Buildings; knowingly causing damage, defacement, structural sabotage, or destruction to real or personal property owned by the state, political subdivisions, river port authorities, state loading docks, or harbor authority complexes.
anchors.subcategory: Willful Vandalism of Public Infrastructure / State Buildings

anchors.phrase: Gambling Fraud / Casino Cheating; winning or attempting to win money or property via a game of chance or wagering track through the use of loaded dice, marked cards, cheating devices, sleight of hand, or fraudulent digital manipulation of electronic gaming kiosks.

Gambling Promotion / Racetrack Bookmaking; knowingly inducing or enticing persons to engage in illegal gambling, or establishing, maintaining, or operating a pool, lottery, racetrack bookmaking ring, or pari-mutuel horse wagering matrix for private or syndicate profit.

Seizure and Forfeiture of Gambling Devices and Proceeds; the statutory seizure and permanent forfeiture to the state of Tennessee of all money, electronic gaming machines, poker tables, communications equipment, vehicles, and real property used in or derived from an illegal gambling operation.

Unlawful Gambling Participation; knowingly making or accepting a bet, playing an unauthorized game of chance for money or property, or participating in an un-permitted lottery scheme as an individual participant.
Unlawful Gambling Operation; owning, managing, or operating a non-permitted game of chance, slot machine ring, commercial dice game, or internet gambling server for commercial profit.

Possession of a Gambling Device or Record; knowingly manufacturing, selling, transporting, or possessing any electronic gaming machine, slot machine, video poker apparatus, or written bookkeeping record intended to be utilized in an illegal gambling operation.

Sports Wagering Regulatory Violation; failure of a licensed mobile sports wagering operator or corporate entity to implement required geolocation tracking blocks, verify minor exclusions, preserve bettor logs, or accurately report state privilege tax metrics.

Participant Sports Rigging Enhancement; an active professional or collegiate athlete, coach, official, or trainer knowingly accepting a bribe, intentionally throwing a match, altering points, or manipulating performance metrics to influence the outcome of a publicly exhibited contest for gambling/wagering exploitation.

Judicial Padlocking of a Gambling House Nuisance; a civil chancery court decree ordering the immediate locking, sealing, and physical padlocking of an entire building, commercial space, or real property parcel found to be hosting systemic, illicit gambling promotions or unauthorized lottery networks.

Possession of Commercial Gambling Devices / Records; knowingly manufacturing, selling, transporting, or possessing any slot machine, electronic video lottery terminal, or physical bookmaking betting records with the intent to operate or facilitate commercial gambling promotion.

Possession of Gambling Records for Profit Promotion; possessing, writing, or collecting numbers, slips, tokens, or digital logs designed to record bets or wagers with the explicit intent to profit from managing or promoting a policy game, unauthorized pool, or lottery ring.

Financial Exploitation by Power of Attorney; knowingly using a power of attorney instrument to deceive, intimidate, convert, or deplete the cash, real estate, personal property, or assets of an elderly (60+) or vulnerable adult principal for personal or corporate gain.
anchors.subcategory: Financial Exploitation by Power of Attorney / Guardian

anchors.phrase: Caregiver Financial Exploitation via Undue Influence; knowingly and through undue influence, deception, coercion, or fraud, converting or depleting the financial resources, real estate holdings, or liquid assets of an elderly or vulnerable adult without valid legal instruments like a POA.

Misappropriation of Ward Assets by Conservator/Guardian; a court-appointed conservator or guardian knowingly converting, embezzling, or misappropriating the real estate, cash, or investment assets of a minor or disabled ward for personal or third-party use in violation of court-mandated management parameters.

Fiduciary Accounting Non-Compliance; failure or explicit refusal of a court-appointed guardian, conservator, or administrator to file required annual financial accountings, inventory sheets, or bank statements regarding a ward's real and personal estate after explicit probate court citation.

Tenant Failure to Pay Rent / Lease Non-Compliance; failure of a residential tenant to pay rent within the contractually defined grace period or violating core behavioral/lease terms, triggering a statutory 14-day notice to remediate or vacate under URLTA parameters.

Execution of a Writ of Possession; the mechanical sheriff-enforced physical clearing of real property, removal of a tenant, and restoration of possession to the property owner following a final judicial FED decree and the expiration of the statutory 10-day appeal window.

Deed Fraud and Title Simulation; knowingly making, forging, altering, or uttering a fraudulent real estate deed, property conveyance instrument, or estate title assignment with the intent to record it with a county register of deeds to fraudulently convert property ownership.

Unauthorized Directional Subsurface Drilling and Unlawful Pooling; executing directional drilling, horizontal extraction, or subsurface pooling of oil, gas, or minerals across real property boundary lines without an active state permit, mineral easement, or valid pooling order.

Unlicensed Banking Operation; conducting a banking business, advertising or holding an entity out as a bank, or receiving deposits without a valid state charter or explicit written certificate of authority from the Commissioner of Financial Institutions.

False Banking Book Entries; an officer, director, employee, or agent of a state-chartered bank or trust company knowingly making false entries in bank books, falsifying reports, or misrepresenting financial metrics to deceive TDFI examiners or examiners from the Federal Reserve/FDIC.

Receipt of Deposits During Insolvency; an officer, director, or employee of a bank receiving or permitting the receipt of public deposits when they know or have clear reason to know that the financial institution is insolvent.

Unlicensed Money Transmission; engaging in the business of money transmission, issuing payment instruments, storing value cards, or transferring currency digitally or physically without an active license issued by the Commissioner of Financial Institutions.

Unlicensed Consumer Lending / Industrial Loan Violations; engaging in the business of making consumer loans, equity-linked loans, or charging interest rates exceeding standard state usury caps without an active license registered under the Industrial Loan and Thrift Act framework.

TDFI Civil Money Penalty Assessment; a state-level final order levying non-criminal monetary penalties against a bank or trust company director for violating a final cease-and-desist order, maintaining unsafe or unsound lending concentrations, or ignoring prompt corrective action warnings.

Administrative Non-Compliance / Violation of TDFI Safety and Soundness Rules; violating a written cease-and-desist order, operating with critically deficient capital structures, failing to comply with prompt corrective action mandates, or submitting false reports to the Commissioner of Financial Institutions.

Personal Fiduciary Liability Assessment of Bank Directors; a state-ordered civil surcharge or personal financial assessment levied directly against a bank director or executive officer for gross negligence, intentional violation of lending limits, or permitting unsafe insider loan practices that result in loss to the institution.

Misapplication of Bank Funds by Fiduciary Officer; an officer, director, employee, or agent of a state-chartered bank or trust company knowingly embezzling, abstracting, misapplying, or willfully diverting any of the bank's currency, funds, assets, or credits with the intent to injure or defraud the institution.

Unregistered Broker-Dealer or Agent Operations; transacting business in Tennessee as a broker-dealer, agent, investment adviser, or investment adviser representative without maintaining an active registration with the Tennessee Securities Division.

Sale of Unregistered Securities; selling or offering to sell any security in the State of Tennessee unless it has an active state registration, is a federally covered security, or qualifies for a lawful exemption.

Employment of Fraudulent Securities Devices; employing any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud, making material misrepresentations, or engaging in a course of business that operates as a fraud or deceit upon any person in connection with the offer, sale, or purchase of a security.

Market Manipulation and Wash Sales; creating a false or misleading appearance of active trading in any security, or false appearance with respect to the market for any such security, via wash sales, matched orders, or artificial volume simulation for the purpose of manipulating asset prices.

Reciprocal Statutory Disqualification Stop-Order; being subject to an order, adjudication, or suspension by another state securities administrator, the SEC, FINRA, or a foreign regulatory authority, triggering an automatic reciprocal state stop-order or license revocation in Tennessee.

Controlling Person Civil Securities Liability; every person who directly or indirectly controls a seller or issuer liable for securities fraud, every partner, officer, or director of such seller, and every broker-dealer or agent who materially aids in the act, being held jointly and severally liable to the investor.

Reciprocal Penny Stock Enforcement Bar; being subject to a federal Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) order revoking registration or barring association under the Penny Stock Reform Act, triggering an automatic cross-jurisdictional license revocation in Tennessee.

Securities Record-Keeping Defiance / Anti-Shredding Violation; willfully failing to maintain mandatory blotters, ledgers, and customer account records, or altering, shredding, concealing, or falsifying broker-dealer accounts to obstruct a lawful state regulatory audit or examination.

Corporate Officer and Director Securities Bar; an administrative order issued by the Commissioner denying, suspending, or revoking an individual's capacity to serve as an officer, director, partner, or principal of any registered issuer or broker-dealer based on prior fraudulent market practices.

Policy Terms Misrepresentation; making, issuing, or circulating any estimate, illustration, circular, or statement misrepresenting the terms, benefits, or advantages of any insurance policy issued or to be issued by a licensed underwriter.

Twisting; making any misrepresentation or incomplete comparison of policies to any person for the purpose of inducing, or tending to induce, the person to lapse, forfeit, surrender, retain, exchange, or convert any insurance policy.

Systemic Unfair Claims Settlement Practices; executing general business practices that include misrepresenting pertinent policy facts, refusing to pay claims without conducting a reasonable investigation, failing to affirm or deny coverage within a reasonable time, or compelling insureds to institute litigation to recover amounts due.

Statutory Insurance Bad Faith Civil Penalty; an insurance company refusing to pay a loss within sixty (60) days after a demand has been made by the holder of the policy, where such refusal is judicially determined to be not in good faith.

Fraudulent Asset Transfer Prior to Insolvency; an officer, director, or parent entity executing transfers of an insurance company's assets, reserves, or real property within 1 year prior to the filing of a petition for rehabilitation or liquidation, with the intent to defraud, delay, or hinder policyholders, creditors, or the Commissioner.

Reciprocal Out-of-State Asset Clawback Violation; an out-of-state insurer or holding company failing to comply with an ancillary receiver's demand or court order in Tennessee to return localized assets, premium reserves, or property belonging to a domiciliary insurer undergoing liquidation in another state.

Statutory Scope and Definition of Fraudulent Insurance Acts; establishing the specific legal boundaries for masking, concealment, or systemic accounting deceptions within an insurer's capital structures, which serves as the predicate trigger for multi-agency enforcement overlays.

Fraudulent Concealment from Insurance Commissioner; knowingly hiding, masking, or failing to disclose material financial or structural operational details requested by the Commissioner of Commerce and Insurance during an inspection, audit, or rehabilitation review.

Unlicensed Practice of Public Accountancy; performing attest or compilation services, or using the titles "Certified Public Accountant," "CPA," "Public Accountant," or any abbreviations intended to deceive the public into believing the individual or firm holds an active certificate issued by the Tennessee State Board of Accountancy.

Accountant Falsification of Financial Statements / Auditing Forgery; knowingly making, certifying, or presenting a false financial report, balance sheet, or audit statement, or willfully altering corporate ledger entries to mask institutional asset defalcation, evade state/federal revenue tracking, or inflate public corporate metrics.

Accountancy Confidentiality Breach / Board Investigative Obstruction; willfully disclosing confidential client financial data without authorization outside statutory legal vectors, or refusing to supply mandatory audit workpapers, records, or testimony requested by the TBA during a formal regulatory discipline or fraud check.

Peer Review Deception and Non-Compliance; a licensed accountancy firm or managing partner failing to undergo required state-mandated peer reviews, or knowingly submitting falsified, altered, or deceptive peer review compliance documentation to the Board to preserve firm permit certifications.

Contingent Fee Exploitation and Illicit Commission Acceptance; a CPA or public accountancy firm soliciting, receiving, or paying a prohibited contingent fee or referral commission while performing attest services (such as audits, compilations, or examinations of prospective financial records) where professional independence is statutorily required.

Unlicensed Practice of Architecture or Engineering; practicing, offering to practice, or using titles intended to deceive the public into believing the individual holds an active registration issued by the Board.

Professional Stamp/Seal Misuse; a registered architect, engineer, or landscape architect stamping, sealing, or signing plans, blueprints, or specifications that were not prepared by the registrant or under their direct, personal, and responsible supervisory control.

Professional Misconduct / Malpractice of Architects or Engineers; engaging in gross negligence, incompetence, or misconduct in the practice of architecture or engineering, practicing on an expired license, or violating the Board's rules of professional conduct.

Unlicensed Real Estate Brokerage; acting as a real estate broker, affiliate broker, timeshare salesperson, or property manager, or advertising or holding oneself out as such, without first obtaining an active license from the Tennessee Real Estate Commission.

Unlicensed Real Estate Commission Treble Penalty; receiving money, compensation, or a commission as a consequence of acting as an unlicensed broker or salesperson, triggering an automatic civil penalty in a court of competent jurisdiction.

Professional Broker Misconduct / License Violations; making material misrepresentations, pursuing a continued course of misrepresentation through affiliate brokers, failing to timely remit escrow or trust funds, or commingling personal funds with client real estate deposits.

Escrow Account Commingling and Trust Account Defiance; failure of a principal broker to maintain a separate, distinct, and audited escrow or trust account for earnest money deposits, or willfully commingling client real estate trust funds with personal or corporate operational accounts.

Real Estate Equity Theft and Deceptive Conveyance; a licensed or unlicensed real estate actor utilizing deceptive trade representations, straw buyers, or falsified closing summaries to execute fraudulent equity skimming, unauthorized title conversion, or theft of consumer earnest property.

Unlicensed Commercial or Residential Contracting; undertaking, bidding, or offering to undertake the construction, alteration, or repair of any building, highway, or structure where the total project value equals or exceeds $25,000 ($3,000 for masonry; $25,000 for roofing/HVAC) without holding an active contractor's license issued by the Board.

Contractor Civil Fraud and Material Misrepresentation; knowingly providing false financial state metrics, forging bonding documentation, or executing fraudulent sworn statements regarding lien waivers or subcontractor payouts to the Board, financial institutions, or consumers to secure project funds or certification.

Professional Contractor Misconduct / Code Violations; engaging in gross negligence, financial structural abandonment of a project, bidding outside authorized monetary classification limits, or violating local building code rules resulting in active public safety hazards.

Unpermitted Combat Sports Promotion; promoting, staging, conducting, or participating in any professional boxing, sparring, martial arts, or mixed martial arts (MMA) match or public exhibition without securing an active promoter license or event permit from the Tennessee Athletic Commission.

Conducting Unpermitted Combat Sports Event; promoting, staging, or conducting a professional boxing, sparring, martial arts, or mixed martial arts (MMA) event without securing an active, valid event permit and matching municipal bond clearance sheets from the Tennessee Athletic Commission.

Athletic Commission Civil Non-Compliance; violating mandatory fighter medical clearance rules, failing to provide required ringside physician structures, or exceeding approved spectator limits at a combat sports exhibition.

Unlicensed Auctioneer Promotion; calling, crying, guiding, or bidding at a public asset liquidation, real estate auction, or commercial auto sale, or advertising an auction, without first holding a valid active license from the Tennessee Auctioneer Commission.

Unlicensed Barbering Operations; practicing the occupation of barbering, cutting or shaving hair for compensation, or operating a commercial barber shop facility without first holding an active certificate of registration from the Tennessee Board of Barber Examiners.

Unlicensed Private Security Operations; acting as an armed or unarmed security guard, patrol service operator, or proprietary security organization without holding an active registration card or contract company license issued by the Commissioner of Commerce and Insurance.

Unlawful Armed Guard Posting; working as an armed guard or posting armed security personnel who do not possess a valid firearm registration card or specific calibre endorsement, or carrying an unauthorized or non-registered type of weapon while on active contractual duty.

Unlicensed Private Investigation; engaging in the business of private investigation, conducting commercial surveillance, or tracking individuals for hire without holding an active investigator license or company agency manager license.

Investigative Ethics Violation and Surveillance Abuse; a licensed investigator utilizing illegal wiretaps, hidden tracking bugs without owner consent, or committing fraud, extortion, or deliberate material misrepresentation while executing assignment monitoring.
Unlicensed Locksmith Operations; practicing locksmithing, marketing lock re-keying services, or replicating restricted master key codes without an active license from the Locksmith Licensing Program.

Unlicensed Real Estate Appraisal; performing real estate appraisals, executing commercial evaluation charts, or issuing certified property valuations for banking lending purposes without holding an active registration or certification from the Real Estate Appraiser Commission.

Real Estate Appraisal Falsification / Certified Misconduct; knowingly developing, signing, or certifying a fraudulent real estate appraisal report containing material misrepresentations, or manipulating market data comparisons to artificially inflate or deflate real property values for mortgage financing exploitation.

Appraiser Regulatory Violation / USPAP Non-Compliance; failing to adhere to the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP), practicing on an expired or inactive registration, or failing to maintain required workfiles for state review.

Scrap Metal Dealer Record Violations / Purchasing Stolen Copper; failing to log required identification, fingerprints, or vehicle data from sellers, or purchasing regulated metals (such as copper wire, catalytic converters, or municipal grates) with reason to believe the items are stolen or illegally converted property.

Unlicensed Practice of Soil Science / Fraudulent Survey Reporting; practicing as a soil scientist, certifying environmental impact maps, or using the title "Licensed Professional Soil Scientist" without a valid active license from the Department, or knowingly falsifying soil absorption or compaction metrics to clear real estate zoning hurdles.

Purchasing Stolen Critical Infrastructure Scrap; a registered dealer knowingly or recklessly buying stolen HVAC copper coils, municipal water meters, electrical grid cabling, or highway markers, bypassing recording bars to conceal the illicit origin of property.

Unlicensed Practice of Soil Science; practicing, offering to practice, or utilizing the title "Licensed Professional Soil Scientist" on municipal environmental health documents or agricultural drainage plans without an active certificate issued by the Department of Commerce and Insurance.

Unlicensed Practice of Medicine; practicing medicine, surgery, or obstetrics, diagnosing ailments, prescribing pharmaceuticals, or utilizing titles intended to deceive the public into believing the individual holds an active license from the Tennessee Board of Medical Examiners.

Unlicensed Practice of Podiatric Medicine; practicing podiatry, performing unapproved foot/ankle surgical maneuvers, or holding oneself out as a podiatrist (DPM) without a valid license from the Tennessee Board of Podiatric Medical Examiners.

Unlawful Corporate Practice of Podiatry; a corporation, partnership, or limited liability company (LLC) offering or rendering professional podiatric services or ankle surgeries without a designated registered practitioner in responsible charge, or failing to maintain a valid facility disclosure listing with the Board.

Unlicensed Practice of Veterinary Medicine; practicing veterinary medicine, surgery, or dentistry on animals, diagnosing animal conditions, prescribing veterinary drugs, or using matching titles without an active license from the Board.

Unlawful Veterinary Narcotics Dispensing; an uncertified veterinary assistant or technician compounding, dispensing, or executing automated delivery of controlled substances or dangerous animal pharmaceuticals without the explicit direct supervision of a licensed veterinarian.

Professional Veterinary Misconduct / Ketamine-Euthanasia Diversion; engaging in chronic substance dependency, diverting veterinary narcotics (such as ketamine, tramadol, or euthanasia solutions) to human use, or executing gross malpractice leading to unnecessary animal destruction or torture.

Professional Therapy Misconduct / Patient Boundary Violation; violating therapist-client sexual or financial boundaries, exploiting vulnerable psychiatric clients, executing deceptive billing practices under corporate therapy networks, or being convicted of standard felonies.

Unlicensed Social Work Practice; practicing, offering to practice, or using titles such as "Licensed Clinical Social Worker," "LCSW," or "Licensed Master Social Worker" without holding an active certificate or license issued by the Board.
