Social Work Professional Misconduct / Patient Exploitation; engaging in unprofessional conduct, gross negligence, boundary violations, or exploiting a clinically vulnerable patient for personal, emotional, or financial gain while licensed as a social worker.

Nursing Home Administrator Misconduct / Facility Neglect; engaging in gross negligence, financial malfeasance, fraud, or violating safe facility staffing rules, causing direct patient care endangerment, systemic neglect, or institutional abuse conditions within a licensed long-term care facility.

Unlicensed Practice as a Nursing Home Administrator; practicing, acting, or holding oneself out as a nursing home administrator, or managing a long-term care facility without an active, valid license issued by the Board of Examiners for Nursing Home Administrators.

Unlicensed Mental Health Practice; advertising, offering, or providing professional counseling, marital and family therapy, or utilizing protected titles (LPC, LMFT) without holding an active registration from the state board.

Willful Attempt to Evade or Defeat State Taxes; willfully attempting in any manner to evade or defeat any tax imposed by the Tennessee Revenue Title, or filing a false or fraudulent state tax return (Sales, Franchise, Excise, or Mixed Drink taxes).

Criminal Usury; collecting, contracting for, or receiving interest at a rate that exceeds the maximum statutory formula rates willfully and knowingly, typically run through unlicensed lending, cash syndicates, or predatory operations.

Willful Failure to Perform Duty under Unclaimed Property Act; willfully failing to report, pay, or deliver abandoned assets to the Tennessee State Treasurer after the legal statutory dormancy timeframe expires.

Willful Violations of Consumer Credit / Financing Disclosures; willfully making false statements or failing to provide required financing disclosures regarding rates, loan parameters, or fees, or failing to comply with rules under the Tennessee Industrial Loan and Thrift framework.

Extortion; knowingly utilizing an implicit or explicit threat to cause bodily injury, property damage, reputational harm, or dynamic economic leverage to compel another person to hand over money, goods, or services.

Failure to Collect, Account For, or Pay Over Trust Taxes; willfully failing to collect, truthfully account for, and pay over state trust taxes (such as retail sales tax or employee withholding taxes).

Money Laundering; knowingly conducting, supervising, or facilitating a financial transaction involving property or currency derived from specified criminal activities with the intent to promote the criminal enterprise or conceal the nature, location, source, ownership, or control of the proceeds.

Willful Failure to File Return or Supply Information; willfully failing to pay an estimated tax, make a return, keep required commercial records, or supply tracking information to the Department of Revenue at the time required by law.

Willful Making of False Statement Under Perjury Penalties; willfully making and signing any state tax return, report, statement, or other document verified by a written declaration that it is made under the penalties of perjury, which the maker does not believe to be true and correct as to every material matter.

Forfeiture of Usurious Interest and Contract Invalidation; entering into a lending arrangement that charges interest exceeding the maximum statutory limits, triggering mandatory civil forfeiture of all excess interest and providing the debtor the right to void the underlying loan contract entirely.

Compounding Administrative Penalties and Interest for Unclaimed Property Non-Compliance; the structured civil mechanism utilized by the State Treasurer to enforce compliance against corporate holders who fail to report, pay, or deliver abandoned property within the statutory timeframe.

Issuance and Execution of Arrest Warrant; the formal judicial process where a magistrate or judge finds probable cause via written affidavit that a financial felony has occurred, authorizing law enforcement to seize the person and transport them to a secure intake facility.

Criminal Summons in Lieu of Warrant; the statutory procedural mandate requiring judges or clerks to issue a written summons instead of a physical arrest warrant for non-felony violations, ordering the individual to appear for booking and arraignment on a set date without custodial capture.

Seizure Warrant / Forfeiture Order Requirement; the mandatory judicial process requiring law enforcement to obtain a forfeiture warrant from a judge within five business days of seizing any cash, bank accounts, vehicles, or ledger hardware linked to a money laundering or usury syndicate.

Notice of Charge and Booking Restrictions / Telephone Rule; the mandatory statutory restriction prohibiting booking desk personnel from listing an arrestee's name in any public log until the individual has been given one hour to successfully complete a phone call to an attorney, family member, or bond agent.

Mandatory Inmate Intake Record Keeping; the administrative regulation governing all local county jails across Tennessee, forcing the booking clerk to immediately collect and lock explicit metadata into the local system: mugshot, booking number, exact numeric statutory charge code, automated finger scan, and warrant source data.
Use of Citations in Lieu of Continued Custodial Arrest; the operational framework governing when an officer can bypass physical jail transport for misdemeanors.

Engaging in Unlicensed Money Transmission; willfully engaging in the business of money transmission, running cash operations, or cash-skimming syndicates without obtaining an explicit operating license from the Tennessee Department of Financial Institutions.

Using False Exemption Certificates or Operating Without a Resale Certificate; using a faked, altered, or fraudulent resale certificate to buy commercial goods without paying retail sales tax, or operating a retail establishment without displaying a current, valid certificate of registration from the Department of Revenue.
Aggravated Rape; unlawful sexual penetration accompanied by force, weapon, or causing bodily injury.
Rape; unlawful sexual penetration without consent via force, coercion, or intoxication.
Sexual Battery; unlawful sexual contact with a victim via force, coercion, or lack of consent.
Violation of the Tennessee Sex Offender Registry Act; failure to register, verify address, or reporting false information.
Unlawful photography; capturing images of an individual's private areas without consent, now requiring mandatory registration.
Criminal Arrest Warrant issued by a magistrate or judge for an underlying sex offense charge.
Civil Order of Protection issued based on a judicial finding of sexual assault or abuse.
Aggravated Rape of a Child (Adult Offender); unlawful sexual penetration of a victim by an adult defendant (or vice versa) where the victim is eight (8) years of age or less.
Aggravated Rape of a Child (Juvenile Offender); unlawful sexual penetration of a victim eight (8) years of age or less, where the perpetrator is legally a juvenile.

Especially Aggravated Rape of a Child (Adult Offender); unlawful sexual penetration of a child victim accompanied by three or more statutory aggravating factors (e.g., torture, mutilation, kidnapping).
Aggravated Sexual Battery; unlawful sexual contact (touching or fondling) with a child victim who is less than thirteen (13) years of age.

Continuous Sexual Abuse of a Child (Class A); engaging in three (3) or more separate acts of sexual abuse against a child victim over a duration of time, where the underlying acts include Aggravated Rape of a Child or Rape of a Child.
Accessory after the fact; intentionally harboring, concealing, providing means of escape, or warning a known felon to hinder arrest, trial, conviction, or punishment.
Aggravated prostitution; engaging in sexual activity as a business, or acting as an inmate in a house of prostitution while knowing oneself to be infected with HIV.
Aggravated rape of a child; unlawful sexual penetration of a child victim three (3) years of age or less.
Aggravated sexual battery; unlawful sexual contact accompanied by force, weapons, injury, or where the victim is less than thirteen (13) years of age.

Aggravated sexual exploitation of a minor; knowingly promoting, selling, distributing, transporting, or purchasing material that includes a minor engaged in sexual activity or simulated sexual activity.

Aggravated Spousal Rape; unlawful sexual penetration of one spouse by the other, accompanied by especially cruel, vile, and inhumane conduct, where the defendant is armed with a weapon or causes serious bodily injury.
Assault with intent to commit or attempt to commit rape; an assaultive act executed with the specific concurrent intent to accomplish unlawful sexual penetration.
Assault with intent to commit or attempt to commit sexual battery; an assault executed with the explicit intent to accomplish unlawful sexual contact (touching or fondling).
Criminal attempt; intentionally engaging in an action or taking a substantial step that would constitute a criminal offense if circumstances were as believed.
Criminal conspiracy; an agreement between two or more people to engage in conduct constituting an offense, accompanied by an overt act in pursuance of the agreement.
Criminal exposure to HIV; knowingly engaging in intimate contact with another individual while knowing oneself to be infected with HIV.

Criminal sexual conduct in the first degree; engaging in sexual penetration with a victim who is twelve (12) years of age or under, or less than sixteen (16) years of age if the actor is a relative or holds custodial/official authority.

Criminal sexual conduct in the second degree; engaging in unlawful sexual contact with a victim under thirteen (13) years of age, or a victim between 13 and 16 years of age under blood relation or custodial authority.

Criminal sexual conduct in the third degree; engaging in sexual penetration with a victim aged 13 to 16 where the actor is at least 3 years older, or utilizing force/coercion, or under the pretext of a medical examination.

Especially aggravated kidnapping; false imprisonment accomplished with a deadly weapon, where the victim was under thirteen (13) years of age, or held for ransom/hostage, or suffers serious bodily injury.

Especially Aggravated Sexual Exploitation of a Minor; knowingly promoting, employing, using, assisting, transporting, or permitting a minor to participate in the performance or production of obscene sexual materials.

Soliciting Sexual Exploitation of a Minor / Electronic Exploitation; an adult intentionally commanding, inducing, or causing a minor via electronic, oral, or written means to engage in sexual or simulated sexual activity observed by the person.

Electronic Solicitation / Forced Exposure to a Minor; intentionally displaying or exposing a minor to obscene or patently offensive sexual materials for sexual gratification, or doing so to a law enforcement officer posing as a minor.
Incest; knowingly engaging in sexual penetration with a closely related individual (e.g., parent, child, grandparent, sibling, stepchild, nephew, niece).
Indecent exposure; intentionally exposing genitals/buttocks or engaging in sexual acts publicly, or enticing a child under 13 into a residence for masturbation/exposure.
Kidnapping; false imprisonment under circumstances exposing the victim to a substantial risk of bodily injury, where the victim is a minor and offender is a non-parent.

Promotion of performances including sexual conduct by children; knowingly producing, directing, manufacturing, or selling a performance (play, film, photo, dance) that features actual or simulated sexual conduct by a minor.

Sexual Battery by an Authority Figure; unlawful sexual contact with a victim aged 13 to 17 (or helpless at any age) where the actor leverages a position of trust, supervisory/disciplinary power, or parental/custodial authority.
Solicitation; intentionally commanding, requesting, or hiring another person to commit a criminal offense, with the intent that the offense be committed.

Solicitation of a Minor; an adult intentionally commanding, requesting, or attempting to induce a minor (or law enforcement officer posing as a minor) to engage in specified severe sexual felonies (e.g., child rape, battery, exploitation).
Spousal Rape (Aggravated Variant); unlawful sexual penetration of one spouse by the other where the defendant is armed with a weapon or causes serious bodily injury.
Spousal Rape (Separated Variant); unlawful sexual penetration of one spouse by the other where the spouses are living apart and one has filed for separate maintenance or divorce.
Spousal Sexual Battery (Aggravated Variant); unlawful sexual contact (touching/fondling) of one spouse by the other where the defendant is armed with a weapon or causes serious bodily injury.
Spousal Sexual Battery (Separated Variant); unlawful sexual contact of one spouse by the other where the spouses are living apart and one has filed for separate maintenance or divorce.

Statutory Rape; unlawful sexual penetration of a minor victim aged 13 to 17 where a specified age differential exists between the victim and defendant (e.g., defendant 4–5 years older for mitigated tracks; 4–9 years older for standard minor brackets).

Aggravated Statutory Rape; unlawful sexual penetration of a victim who is at least thirteen (13) but less than eighteen (18) years of age, where the defendant is at least ten (10) years older than the victim.
Especially Aggravated Rape of a Child; unlawful sexual penetration of a child under 13 accompanied by severe aggravating factors such as torture, serious injury, or use of a deadly weapon.
Prostitution; engaging in sexual activity as a business for hire. (Note: Victims under 18 are immune from prosecution and diverted to services).

Patronizing Prostitution; soliciting or hiring another person with the intent to engage in prostitution, or entering/remaining in a brothel. (Note: If the person patronized is under 18, it is punished under severe human trafficking laws).
Promoting Prostitution; owning, managing, or supervising a prostitution business/house, or procuring inmates/prostitutes for patrons. (Excludes self-promotion).

Continuous Sexual Abuse of a Child (Class B Variant); engaging in multiple acts of sexual abuse against a child victim over a period where exactly two of the acts constitute the severe offenses listed in subdivision (c)(1).
Prostitution within Prohibited Zone; engaging in prostitution within 100 feet of a church or within 1.5 miles of a school.

Trafficking for a Commercial Sex Act (Minor Victim); knowingly recruiting, enticing, harboring, or obtaining a child under eighteen (18) years of age for the purpose of a commercial sex act. (Note: Force or coercion is not required to prove this offense if the victim is a minor).

Indecent Exposure (Residential Minor Enticement); an adult (18+) knowingly inviting, enticing, or fraudulently inducing a minor aged 13 to 17 into a private residence to expose genitals/buttocks or masturbate without consent for sexual gratification.

Possession or Production of Technology/AI Tools for Child Exploitation; knowingly possessing, distributing, or producing software, technology, or digital tools designed or modified by artificial intelligence for the purpose of creating material depicting a minor engaged in sexual activity.
Violation of Community Supervision for Life; knowingly violating a restriction or condition of lifetime supervision (e.g., entering a school zone, failing a polygraph, tracking device manipulation).

Child Abuse and Neglect (Baseline); knowingly, other than by accidental means, treating a child under eighteen (18) years of age in such a manner as to inflict injury or neglect affecting the child's health and welfare.
Child Abuse and Neglect (Felony Grade); knowingly treating or neglecting a child where the victim is eight (8) years of age or less.

Aggravated Child Abuse / Aggravated Child Neglect; committing child abuse or neglect as defined in § 39-15-401 that results in serious bodily injury, or involves the use of a deadly weapon, or where the child is eight (8) years of age or less and suffers serious injury.
Criminal Non-Support; a person who intentionally fails to provide support to their minor child or child over 18 who is disabled, when able to provide support.

Contributing to Delinquency, Unruly Conduct, or Dependency; any adult who, by any act or omission, intentionally contributes to, encourages, or tends to cause any child to become unruly, delinquent, or dependent and neglected.
anchors.subcategory: Contributing to Delinquency of a Minor

anchors.phrase: Elder / Vulnerable Adult Abuse (Knowing Abuse); knowingly abusing an adult who is eighteen (18) years of age or older and who is unable to manage their own resources, carry out activities of daily living, or protect themselves from neglect, harm, or exploitation due to mental or physical dysfunction.
Aggravated Elder / Vulnerable Adult Abuse; knowingly abusing a vulnerable adult where the abuse results in serious bodily injury, permanent disability, or severe mental injury.
Elder / Vulnerable Adult Neglect; knowingly or intentionally neglecting a vulnerable adult, by a caretaker, resulting in a substantial risk of physical harm, deterioration, or illness.

Financial Exploitation of an Adult; knowingly using deception, intimidation, or undue influence to obtain, use, or control the property, assets, or resources of an elderly or vulnerable adult for personal benefit or profit.
Violation of an Order of Protection; knowingly violating a valid, served order of protection issued by a court of competent jurisdiction (including ex parte orders or foreign protection orders).

Aggravated Violation of an Order of Protection; knowingly violating a valid protection order while in possession of a deadly weapon, or committing an aggregate act of domestic assault or stalking during the violation event window.

Nonsupport of a Child; intentionally or knowingly failing to provide necessary food, shelter, clothing, or medical care for a minor child, or failing to comply with a lawful court order of child support when financially capable of performance.

Abuse of an Elderly or Vulnerable Adult; knowingly inflicting physical harm, severe mental anguish, or unreasonable physical confinement upon an adult who is 60 years of age or older or who suffers from a physical or mental dysfunction.

Aggravated Abuse of an Elderly or Vulnerable Adult; knowingly abusing an elderly or vulnerable adult where the act results in serious bodily injury, involves a deadly weapon, or is committed via heinous, atrocious, or cruel means involving torture.

Sexual Exploitation of an Elderly or Vulnerable Adult; a caregiver or person in a position of trust knowingly engaging in sexual contact, penetration, or generating sexually explicit material featuring an elderly or vulnerable adult.
Child Abuse or Neglect (Age 8 or Less / Bodily Injury); knowingly committing child abuse or neglect against a child who is eight (8) years of age or less, resulting in bodily injury.
Aggravated Child Abuse; knowingly committing child abuse as defined in § 39-15-401(a) where the act results in serious bodily injury to the child.
Aggravated Child Neglect; knowingly committing child neglect as defined in § 39-15-401(b) where the neglectful conduct results in serious bodily injury to the minor victim.
Aggravated Child Abuse or Neglect (Weapon Deployment); knowingly committing child abuse or neglect through the use of a deadly weapon, dangerous instrument, or controlled substance.
Flagrant Non-Support of a Child; a person leaves the state of Tennessee to avoid their lawful support obligation, or fails to comply with a support order while residing out of state.

Domestic Assault; committing an assault as defined in § 39-13-101 against a domestic abuse victim (spouse, former spouse, cohabitant, relative by blood/marriage, or dating partner) causing bodily injury or offensive physical contact.

Domestic Assault (Third or Subsequent Offense); committing a domestic assault where the defendant possesses two or more prior domestic assault convictions within the state of Tennessee or equivalent out-of-state jurisdictions.

Child Support Order Judgment Enforcement and Administrative Interest Accumulation; statutorily mandates that each unpaid monthly child support installment automatically becomes a vested, non-modifiable civil judgment T.C.A. § 36-5-101(f)(1)(A) that accumulates statutory compounding interest and cannot be retroactively reduced or discharged.

Domestic Assault accompanied by Physical Restraint, Blocking Exits, or Strangulation; executing a domestic assault where the actor knowingly restrains the victim's physical movement, prevents exit from a structure, or commits strangulation/attempted strangulation T.C.A. § 39-13-111(f).

Theft of Property / Consolidated Embezzlement ($1,000 or Less); knowingly obtaining or exercising control over property or services without the owner's effective consent, with the intent to deprive the owner of the property. This single statute consolidates embezzlement, larceny, and conversion.

Theft of Property / Consolidated Embezzlement ($250,000 or More); knowingly obtaining or exercising control over high-value corporate, public, or private property or services without effective consent, with intent to deprive.

Bribery of a Public Servant; knowingly offering, conferring, or agreeing to confer any pecuniary benefit upon a public servant, or a public servant soliciting or accepting any benefit, to influence the public servant's vote, opinion, recommendation, or exercise of discretion.

Forgery; knowingly forging a writing with the intent to defraud or harm another. "Forge" includes altering, making, completing, executing, or uttering any writing so that it purports to be the act of another who did not authorize it.

Falsification of Books or Records; altering, destroying, concealing, or falsifying any record, book, document, or writing of a corporation, business entity, association, or government agency with the intent to defraud, deceive, or injure any person or entity.

Official Misconduct; a public servant commits an offense who, with intent to obtain a benefit or to injure or deprive another of a benefit, knowingly commits an unauthorized act under color of office, exceeds their authority, or refrains from performing a mandatory duty.

Racketeer Influenced and Corrupt Organizations Act (RICO) Violation; knowingly conducting or participating in an enterprise through a pattern of racketeering activity, or acquiring/maintaining an interest in an enterprise through such activity, or conspiring to do so.
Aggravated Perjury; committing perjury as defined under § 39-16-702, where the false statement is made in an official proceeding and is material to the issue or adjudication.

RICO Criminal Forfeiture Mandates; requires that upon a racketeering conviction under § 39-12-204, the court shall order the criminal forfeiture of all interests, properties, claims, or contractual rights obtained, maintained, or used to facilitate the enterprise.

Official Oppression; a public servant acting under color of office intentionally subjects another to unlawful arrest, detention, search, seizure, dispossession, assessment, or lien, or denies another the exercise of any legal right.

Criminal Contempt of Court; willful disobedience of any lawful writ, process, order, rule, decree, or command of a Tennessee state court, or misbehavior in the presence of the court to obstruct justice.
Escape from Custody (Misdemeanor / Non-Felon); knowingly escaping from lawful custody when the individual is held on a misdemeanor charge, a misdemeanor conviction, or a local administrative hold.

Escape from Custody (Felon Variant); knowingly escaping from a penal institution, a local jail, or active law enforcement custody when the individual is held on a felony charge or a felony conviction. This captures multi-state classifications for Escape in the First/Second Degree.

Failure to Appear / Bail Jumping (Misdemeanor Track); knowingly failing to appear in court as directed by a lawful administrative citation, criminal summons, or bail release conditions while facing a baseline misdemeanor charge.

Failure to Appear / Bail Jumping (Felony Track); knowingly failing to appear in court as directed by a lawful citation, subpoena, or bail release conditions while facing an active felony charge or following a felony conviction.

False Reports to Law Enforcement; knowingly making a false report or statement to a law enforcement officer, emergency dispatcher, or government investigator concerning the commission of a crime, an emergency, or an identity, with intent to deceive or disrupt operations.
Evading Arrest in a Motor Vehicle; intentionally fleeing or attempting to elude a law enforcement officer after receiving a clear visual or audible signal to stop, while operating a motor vehicle.

Unlawful Use or Disclosure of Criminal Justice Information; a public servant, law enforcement employee, or authorized system user knowingly accesses, uses, or discloses confidential criminal justice information (e.g., NCIC, TIES data, or protected juvenile files) for an unauthorized personal, non-law enforcement, or commercial purpose.

Resisting Arrest; intentionally preventing or obstructing a person known to be a law enforcement officer, or anyone acting in a law enforcement officer's presence and at the officer's direction, from effecting an arrest, search, or stop by using physical force against the law enforcement officer or another.

Evading Arrest on Foot; intentionally fleeing on foot from a person known to be a law enforcement officer, with knowledge or reason to know that the officer is attempting to arrest, stop, or detain the individual.

Evading Arrest in a Motor Vehicle; intentionally fleeing in a motor vehicle from a person known to be a law enforcement officer, with knowledge or reason to know that the officer is attempting to arrest, stop, or detain the individual.

Evading Arrest in a Motor Vehicle (High-Risk Aggravated Variant); flight in a motor vehicle from law enforcement where the flight creates a high risk of death or serious bodily injury to innocent bystanders, pursuing officers, or other motorists.

Civil Rights Violations / Hate Crime Sentencing Enhancement; committing an offense against a person or property (such as Civil Rights Intimidation) maliciously motivated by hatred or animus toward the race, color, religion, ancestry, national origin, or gender of the victim. This acts as a statutory enhancement that elevates the underlying felony or misdemeanor classification by one class T.C.A. § 40-35-114(17).

Violating State Anti-Discrimination or Hate Crime Restraining Orders; knowingly violating a civil injunction, a human rights commission cease-and-desist order, or a specific court order restraining the individual from harassing, discriminating against, or violating the civil rights of a protected party.

Aggravated Arson; committing arson as defined in § 39-14-301 where one or more persons are present in the structure at the time of the offense, or where any person, including firefighters or emergency personnel, suffers serious bodily injury. This captures multi-state terminology for Arson in the First Degree.

Disorderly Conduct; a person commits an offense who, in a public place and with intent to cause public annoyance or alarm, engages in fighting or violent behavior, makes unreasonable noise, or obstructs vehicular or pedestrian traffic. This captures multi-state entries for Disturbing the Peace.

Gambling Promotion; knowingly inducing or aiding another to engage in gambling, and demonstrating a commercial or enterprise intent by managing, operating, or executing an unpermitted or illegal lottery, bingo game, or gambling operation for profit.

Criminal Gang Organization Recruitment; knowingly soliciting, inviting, encouraging, or causing another person to join a criminal gang with the intent to promote or further the gang's criminal objectives.

Riot; knowingly participating in an assemblage of three (3) or more persons which, by tumultuous and violent conduct, creates grave danger of substantial damage to property or serious bodily injury to persons or substantially obstructs law enforcement.

Inciting a Riot; knowingly commanding, soliciting, inciting, or encouraging others to engage in a riot as defined in § 39-17-302 under circumstances where such conduct creates a clear and present danger that a riot will occur.
Criminal Trespass; knowingly entering or remaining on the property of another without effective consent, after receiving notice that entry was forbidden or being requested to leave.

Aggravated Criminal Trespass; entering or remaining on the property of another without effective consent, where the property is a habitable structure, an educational facility, or a public utility facility, and the individual knows or has reason to know that such entry is forbidden.

Vandalism ($1,000 or Less); knowingly causing damage to or destruction of any real or personal property of another without effective consent. This captures multi-state terminology for Criminal Mischief in the Second Degree.

Vandalism / Criminal Mischief ($250,000 or More); knowingly causing extreme damage to, or total destruction of, any real or personal property of another (including large-scale commercial or industrial infrastructure) without effective consent. This captures multi-state high-tier profiles for Criminal Mischief in the First Degree.

Carrying a Weapon on School Property with Intent to Go Armed; possessor possesses or carries any firearm or other weapon on any public or private school campus, grounds, recreation area, athletic field, or educational institution bus with the specific intent to go armed.

Unlawful / Reckless Discharge of a Firearm from a Motor Vehicle; intentionally or knowingly discharging a firearm from within a motor vehicle or across a public highway, creating a substantial risk of death or serious bodily injury to another person.

Possession or Manufacture of Explosives with Destructive Intent; manufacturing, transporting, storing, distributing, or possessing explosive compounds, dangerous ordnance, or incendiary devices with the explicit intent to deploy them to destroy property or harm persons.

Altering, Defacing, or Possessing a Defaced Firearm; intentionally altering, changing, removing, disfiguring, or obliterating the manufacturer's serial number or other identification mark on a firearm, or knowingly possessing a firearm that has been defaced in this manner.

Straw Purchase of a Firearm; knowingly soliciting, persuading, or enticing a licensed firearms dealer to transfer a firearm under circumstances where the purchaser knows they are acquiring the weapon on behalf of a prohibited person (such as a convicted felon or domestic abuser), or providing false identity documentation on background screening forms.

Employment of a Firearm During the Commission of a Dangerous Felony; intentionally employing a firearm during the commission of, or attempt to commit, a statutorily defined dangerous felony (e.g., specific drug trafficking, burglary, or escape offenses).

Employment of a Firearm During a Dangerous Felony (Recidivist Variant); employing a firearm during a dangerous felony where the defendant has a prior conviction for any of the statutorily enumerated dangerous felonies or a prior firearm employment/possession enhancement.

Possession of a Firearm During the Commission of a Dangerous Felony; knowingly possessing a firearm (as opposed to actively employing it) during the commission of, or attempt to commit, a statutorily defined dangerous felony.

Possession of a Firearm During a Dangerous Felony (Recidivist Variant); possessing a firearm during a dangerous felony where the defendant has a prior conviction for any of the statutorily enumerated dangerous felonies or prior firearm violations.

Handgun Use / Possession Enhancement During Generic Felony Execution; possessing or employing a handgun or deadly weapon during any felony that does not fall under the specialized "dangerous felony" definitions of § 39-17-1324. This triggers a mandatory statutory enhancement factor that forces upward range adjustments during sentencing T.C.A. § 40-35-114(9).

Simple Possession or Casual Exchange; knowingly possessing a controlled substance unless obtained directly from a valid prescription, or executing a casual exchange of a small amount of a controlled substance among individuals without commercial intent.

Manufacturing, Delivering, Selling, or Possessing with Intent to Distribute a Schedule I Controlled Substance; knowingly manufacturing, delivering, selling, or possessing with intent to manufacture, deliver, or sell a substance classified under Schedule I (e.g., Heroin, LSD, Psilocybin, MDMA, or analogues).

Manufacturing, Delivering, Selling, or Possessing with Intent (Cocaine/Methamphetamine < 0.5 grams); knowingly manufacturing, delivering, selling, or possessing with intent to distribute Cocaine or Methamphetamine where the total weight involved is less than 0.5 grams.

Manufacturing, Delivering, Selling, or Possessing with Intent (Fentanyl — 15 grams or More); knowingly manufacturing, delivering, selling, or possessing with intent to distribute Fentanyl, carfentanil, or any derivative analogue where the total weight of the mixture is fifteen (15) grams or more.

Manufacturing, Delivering, Selling, or Possessing with Intent (Schedule III); knowingly manufacturing, delivering, selling, or possessing with intent to distribute a substance classified under Schedule III (e.g., Ketamine, Anabolic Steroids, Buprenorphine, Hydrocodone combination products under specific dosage limits).

Manufacturing, Delivering, Selling, or Possessing with Intent (Schedule IV); knowingly manufacturing, delivering, selling, or possessing with intent to distribute a substance classified under Schedule IV (e.g., Alprazolam/Xanax, Diazepam/Valium, Clonazepam/Klonopin, and equivalent benzodiazepines).

Manufacturing, Delivering, Selling, or Possessing with Intent (Schedule V/VI/VII); knowingly manufacturing, delivering, selling, or possessing with intent to distribute substances classified under Schedule V, VI, or VII (e.g., Codeine cough syrups under dosage caps, standard lower-weight synthetic derivatives, or minor commercial chemical compounds).

Large-Scale Marijuana Cultivation, Delivery, or Sale (70 lbs to 300 lbs / 100 to 500 plants); knowingly manufacturing, cultivating, delivering, or selling bulk quantities of Marijuana (classified under Schedule VI) equal to or exceeding seventy (70) pounds but less than three hundred (300) pounds, or cultivating 100 to 500 plants.

Unlawful Use or Possession of Drug Paraphernalia; knowingly using, or possessing with intent to use, drug paraphernalia to plant, propagate, cultivate, grow, harvest, manufacture, compound, convert, produce, process, prepare, test, analyze, pack, repack, store, contain, conceal, inject, ingest, inhale, or otherwise introduce into the human body a controlled substance. (Note: Fentanyl testing strips are statutorily carved out and excluded from this definition under recent harm-reduction legislative updates).

Drug-Free School Zone Violation (Standard Elevation); manufacturing, delivering, selling, or possessing with intent to manufacture, deliver, or sell a controlled substance within 500 feet of the real property boundaries of a public or private elementary school, middle school, secondary school, childcare agency, public library, recreational center, or municipal park.

Drug-Free School Zone Violation (Minor Proximity); committing a zone violation as defined in subsection (b)(1) where the transaction or possession with intent occurs at a time when minors are actively present on the school grounds, inside the childcare agency, or utilizing the recreational center, or where a minor is directly utilized as an agent or targeted as a recipient.

Simple Possession within a Drug-Free School Zone; knowingly possessing a controlled substance for personal use (as defined under § 39-17-418) inside the 500-foot geographic boundary of an educational institution, childcare agency, public library, or public park. This triggers a mandatory sentence enhancement under subsection (f).

Maintaining a Drug Dwelling or Premises; knowingly keeping, maintaining, renting, or leasing any structure, building, room, or real property node used for the purpose of manufacturing, distributing, selling, or storing controlled substances in violation of the state drug control act.

Obtaining Controlled Substances by Fraud, Deceit, or Forgery; acquiring, obtaining, or attempting to procure possession of a controlled substance by misrepresentation, forgery, or alteration of a prescription, or by concealing a material fact or failing to disclose to a practitioner that the individual has received a concurrent controlled substance prescription from another provider (Doctor Shopping).

Manufacture or Delivery of an Imitation Controlled Substance; knowingly manufacturing, distributing, selling, or possessing with intent to distribute a non-controlled substance under representations or packaging parameters that falsely mimic a controlled substance.

Second Degree Murder via Unlawful Fentanyl Distribution; the unlawful killing of another person proximately resulting from the defendant's distribution, sale, or delivery of Fentanyl, carfentanil, or any derivative analogue, where the user ingests the substance and suffers a fatal overdose.

Delivery of a Controlled Substance to a Minor; an adult (18 years of age or older) knowingly manufacturing, delivering, selling, or furnishing any controlled substance to a minor who is at least three (3) years the adult's junior. This captures generic listings for Corrupting Another with Drugs.

Possession of Marijuana with Intent to Sell (Baseline Bulk Volume); knowingly manufacturing, delivering, selling, or possessing with intent to manufacture, deliver, or sell Marijuana (classified under Schedule VI) where the weight involved is not less than one-half (1/2) ounce (14.175 grams) nor more than ten (10) pounds (4,535 grams).

Burglary; entering or remaining concealed in a public or private building, freight car, watercraft, or motor vehicle other than a habitation without the effective consent of the property owner, with the intent to commit a felony, theft, or assault.

Especially Aggravated Burglary; entering or remaining concealed in a habitation or business structure without effective consent, with intent to commit a felony, theft, or assault, where any person lawfully on the premises suffers serious bodily injury. This captures generic counts for Home Invasion.

Joyriding; taking or using the automobile, motorcycle, motorcycle-truck, or other motor vehicle of another without the owner's effective consent, but without the specific intent to permanently deprive the owner of the vehicle (distinguishing it from Grand Theft Auto). This tracks generic listings for Unauthorized Use of a Vehicle.
Robbery; the intentional or knowing theft of property from the person of another by violence or by putting the person in fear. This captures multi-state terminology for Robbery in the Second Degree.

Especially Aggravated Robbery; committing robbery as defined in § 39-13-401 accomplished with a deadly weapon where the victim suffers serious bodily injury. This captures multi-state terminology for Robbery in the First Degree.
Carjacking; the intentional or knowing taking of a motor vehicle from the possession of another by use of a deadly weapon or by force or intimidation.
Fraudulent use of a credit or debit card; using a card knowing it is stolen, forged, expired, or revoked to obtain property or services, graded as theft based on value.

Computer Crimes / Computer Fraud; knowingly accessing a computer, computer network, or database without authorization, or exceeding authorized access, to obtain money, property, or services by fraud, or to alter, damage, or destroy system data.

Insurance Fraud / Fraudulent Insurance Claims; knowingly presenting, preparing, or making any written or oral statement in support of a claim for payment or other benefit pursuant to an insurance policy, knowing that the statement contains false, incomplete, or misleading information concerning any fact material to the claim.

Securities Fraud under the Tennessee Securities Act; knowingly employing any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud, making any untrue statement of a material fact, or engaging in insider trading practices in connection with the offer, sale, or purchase of any security, directly or indirectly.

Deceptive Business Practices; knowingly committing fraud or deception in the course of business, including altering measuring devices, selling less than the represented quantity, delivering adulterated/mislabeled commodities, or making false/misleading statements in advertisements.

False Entries in Fiduciary/Banking Ledgers; an officer, director, agent, or employee of a bank or financial institution knowingly making a false entry in a book, report, or statement of the bank, or intentionally omitting a material entry, with the intent to deceive a banking examiner, official, or public trust registry.

Identity Theft; knowingly obtaining, possessing, transferring, or using personal identifying information of another person without their effective consent, with the intent to commit any unlawful act, obtain credit, goods, services, or medical data, or misrepresent oneself.

Identity Theft Trafficking / Manufacturing; knowingly manufacturing, selling, transferring, distributing, or purchasing the personal identifying data of three (3) or more individuals, or possessing such data with the intent to distribute, sell, or network it for commercial exploitation.

Mail and Package Theft (Porch Piracy Baseline); knowingly taking, stealing, or destroying mail or packages from a delivery porch, mailbox, curtilage, or residential drop-box without effective consent, where the contents are valued at $1,000 or less.

Mail and Package Theft (Bulk or High-Value Felony); knowingly executing a systematic pattern of mail or package theft from residential or commercial drop-boxes, porches, or postal vehicles where the combined value of stolen property exceeds $1,000 thresholds, or targeting critical cargo transits.

Mail and Package Theft / Porch Piracy; knowingly obtaining, destroying, or exercising control over mail, packages, or delivered parcels from a residential porch, mailbox, delivery drop box, or curtilage without effective consent.

Unlawful Compensation for Official Behavior / Public Corruption; knowingly offering, giving, or agreeing to give any plastic or pecuniary benefit to a public servant, or a public servant soliciting or accepting a benefit, for having exercised their official power or performed their duty in a specific manner.

Unsworn Falsification to Authorities; knowingly making a false statement, or creating a false document, with the intent to mislead a public servant in the performance of their official duty, when the statement or document is submitted pursuant to an application or system requirement.

Failure to File Statement of Financial Interest; a candidate for public office or an elected official fails to file a comprehensive, mandatory disclosure statement detailing sources of income, investments, corporate positions, and potential conflicts of interest within the statutory timeline enforced by the Tennessee Ethics Commission.

Official Conflict of Interest / Public Procurement Evasion; an elected or appointed public official, municipal manager, or state employee overseer who possesses a direct or indirect pecuniary interest in a public contract, purchase, or transaction over which they exercise regulatory or supervisory authority, and fails to disclose or step aside from that transaction.

Mail and Package Theft / Porch Piracy; knowingly taking, destroying, or intercepting mail, packages, or delivered parcels from a residential porch, mailbox, delivery drop box, or the immediate curtilage of a home without effective consent.

Fraudulent Use of a Credit or Debit Card (Device Tracking Specification); a person uses a credit or debit card for the purpose of obtaining property, credit, services, or anything of value, knowing that the card is stolen, forged, expired, or revoked. This represents the explicit standalone tracking mechanism for consumer transaction devices.
Subornation of Perjury; knowingly procuring, inducing, or causing another person to commit perjury or aggravated perjury as defined by state law.

Sale of Unregistered Securities; knowingly offering or selling any security in the state of Tennessee unless it is registered under the Tennessee Securities Act, is an exempt security, or is a covered security under federal rules.

Destruction or Falsification of Governmental Records; knowingly making a false entry in, or false alteration of, a governmental record, or destroying, concealing, removing, or otherwise impairing the verity or availability of a governmental record.

Theft of Services; knowingly obtaining services (e.g., labor, professional services, public utilities, transportation, lodging, or restaurant food) by deception, fraud, coercion, stealth, or mechanical tampering, with the intent to avoid payment.

Theft of Trade Secrets; knowingly stealing, appropriating, copying, drawing, photographing, or transmitting a trade secret without authorization, with the intent to deprive or withhold the control or economic value of that trade secret from its owner.

Unlawful Use of Official Position / Official Misconduct; a public servant knowingly receives or attempts to receive any benefit under color of office or position that the public servant knows is not authorized by law, with the intent to obtain a benefit or to harm or defraud another.

Organized Scheme to Defraud / Deceptive Business Schemes; executing a systematic, ongoing course of conduct or business practice with the intent to defraud multiple victims or obtain property or services from them by false pretenses, fraudulent representations, or deceptive operations.

Identity Theft / Wholesale Identity Theft Ring Operations; obtaining, possessing, transferring, buying, or selling personal identifying data of three (3) or more separate victims, or using multiple stolen identities concurrently within a structured criminal enterprise to facilitate corporate fraud, financial device replication, or credit generation.

Misappropriation of Public Funds / Official Misconduct; a public servant, with intent to obtain a benefit or to harm or defraud another, intentionally commits an act relating to the servant's office or employment that constitutes an unauthorized exercise, use, conversion, or allocation of public funds or government property.

Unsworn Falsification on an Official Regulatory Application (Enhanced Variant); knowingly executing a material unsworn falsification to a state department or regulatory authority specifically to secure an official licensing permission, corporate certification, or commercial permit. This triggers a mandatory statutory enhancement factor that forces maximum localized misdemeanor sentencing tiers or range elevations.

Aggravated Assault; intentionally or knowingly committing an assault as defined in § 39-13-101 that results in serious bodily injury to another, results in the death of another, or involves the use or display of a deadly weapon. This captures multi-state entries for Assault in the First/Second Degree and Felonious Assault.
Reckless Endangerment; knowingly engaging in conduct that places or may place another person in imminent danger of death or serious bodily injury.

Intimidation / Civil Rights Intimidation; an adult intentionally compelling another to perform or abstain from a lawful act through the deployment of injury threats, legal accusations, or economic retaliation metrics, or maliciously damaging property to intimidate an individual based on bias markers. This captures generic listings for Menacing and Intimidation with a Dangerous Weapon.

Stalking; intentionally engaging in a willful course of conduct directed at a specific person that would cause a reasonable person to feel terrorized, frightened, intimidated, threatened, harassed, or molested, and that actually causes the victim to feel as such. This captures multi-state entries for Stalking in the Second Degree.

Harassment; intentionally threatening, alarming, or terrorizing another person by placing telephone calls, sending electronic communications (e.g., text messages, emails, social media transmissions), or writing anonymous letters without legitimate purpose.

Hazing; an intentional or reckless act by a student or organization member directed against any other student, required as an element of initiation or affiliation into an institutional, student, or fraternity network, that causes or creates a substantial risk of physical or mental harm, distress, or humiliation.

Falsely Reporting an Act of Terrorism / Terroristic Threatening; knowingly making a false report, communicating a false threat, or spreading false information concerning an active or impending act of terrorism, mass destruction, or radiological/chemical deployment, knowing that the report or threat is false. This encapsulates multi-state labels for Terroristic Threatening.

Placing a Hoax Device / Hoax Acts of Terrorism; knowingly manufacturing, placing, sending, or deploying an object or device that is designed, constructed, or packaged to mimic or reasonably appear to be a weapon of mass destruction, chemical weapon, explosive device, or radioactive compound, with the intent to cause terror, public alarm, evacuation, or severe distress.

Criminal Gang Activity Prevention Multiplier; committing a criminal offense while actively participating as a documented criminal gang member, with the specific intent to promote, further, or assist in any criminal conduct by gang members. This acts as a statutory mechanism that elevates the underlying felony or misdemeanor classification by one class tier.

Unlawful Assembly; an assemblage of three (3) or more persons with intent to engage in conduct which constitutes a riot, or an assembly which intends to carry out a common purpose in such a manner as to cause a reasonable person to fear that the assembly will commit a breach of the peace or provoke others to do so.

Computer Data Manipulation Fraud; accessing any computer system, network, or server network specifically to execute data tampering, insert fraudulent commands, or systematically manipulate financial logs for corporate or personal gain.

Public Procurement Collusion and Price-Fixing; entering into any contract, combination, or conspiracy to restrain trade or commerce in public works bidding, including bid-rigging, complimentary bidding schemes, market allocation, or unlawful kickback arrangements on public contracts.

Federal Procurement Debarment; a formal determination by a federal debarring official that a contractor/company is "non-responsible" and lacks business integrity due to a conviction for fraud, antitrust violations, embezzlement, theft, tax evasion, or a documented history of severe contract performance default.

Federal Procurement Suspension; an immediate, temporary exclusion of a contractor or company from the federal marketplace by a debarring official, executed based on an active criminal indictment or adequate evidence of serious fraud, collusion, or systemic non-responsibility, pending the completion of a formal investigation or legal proceeding.

GSA SAM System Exclusion Registration; the central, master administrative vehicle utilized to record, display, and execute procurement and non-procurement default exclusions across the entire federal government, ensuring that persons/entities listed are blocked from receiving federal grants, loans, benefits, or contract payouts.

HUD Non-Procurement Debarment; a formal administrative determination by the Department of Housing and Urban Development excluding a contractor, developer, or landlord from participating in HUD programs due to systemic violations of the Fair Housing Act, equity diversion, or mortgage fraud.

USDA Non-Procurement Exclusion and SNAP Retailer Disqualification; an administrative determination by the Department of Agriculture excluding a commercial vendor, food processing entity, or retail store from federal agricultural programs due to food safety defaults, continuous inspection violations, or trafficking in Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) benefits.

Corporate Procurement Fraud Debarment; an administrative exclusion of a corporation or defense contractor from bidding on or receiving public works awards, executed by an agency debarring official based on a preponderance of the evidence demonstrating systemic corporate fraud, billing inflation, product substitution, or defective pricing.

Systemic SAM Fraud Exclusion; a centralized administrative listing within the System for Award Management (SAM) based on a government-wide coordination action, indicating that an individual or entity has been excluded due to a documented pattern of systemic civil fraud, corporate shell setups, or evasion of previous agency debarments.
Intentionally providing the physical means or participating in a physical act by which another person commits or attempts suicide
Killing an individual without lawful justification while intending to kill or do great bodily harm or knowing such acts create a strong probability of death
Custodial arrest or formal charging instrument filed for First Degree Murder resulting in a non-conviction outcome
Causing the death of an unborn child without lawful justification while intending to kill or cause great bodily harm to the pregnant mother or unborn child
Unintentionally killing an individual without lawful justification where acts whether lawful or unlawful are likely to cause death or great bodily harm and are performed recklessly
Killing an individual while under a sudden and intense passion resulting from serious provocation, or acting under an unreasonable belief that self-defense justification existed

A licensed healthcare provider knowingly or intentionally providing the precise medical mechanism or prescribing lethal compounds to execute an unauthorized assisted suicide outside regulatory boundary limits
Unintentionally killing an individual while driving a vehicle, operating an aircraft, or managing watercraft recklessly in a manner likely to cause death or great bodily harm
Committing acts that constitute first-degree murder but where mitigating factors are proven, specifically an intense passion from serious provocation or an imperfect belief in self-defense
Operating a motor vehicle under the influence of alcohol, drugs, or intoxicating compounds where the violation was a proximate cause of a death of one or more persons
Custodial arrest or formal charging instrument filed for Aggravated DUI involving death resulting in an acquittal, dismissal, or dropped charges
Committing or attempting to commit a forcible felony other than second degree murder, during the course of which an individual is killed
Filming, photographing, producing, disseminating, or possessing films, videotapes, photographs, or other depictions of children under 18 engaged in sexual acts
Custodial arrest or formal charging instrument filed for Child Pornography resulting in a non-conviction outcome
Committing an act of sexual penetration with a victim who was under 13 years of age when the act was committed
Criminal sexual abuse involving a minor victim where the record has been legally sealed via specific statutory relief or historical juvenile tracking integration
Committing an act of sexual penetration by the use of force or threat of force, or when the victim is unable to give consent
Performing an act of sexual penetration, sexual conduct, or lewd exposure of the body in a public place with the intent to arouse or satisfy sexual desire
An individual 17 years of age or older committing an act of sexual penetration with a victim who was under 13 years of age
Knowingly recruiting, harboring, transporting, providing, or obtaining another person for the purpose of commercial sexual activity through force, fraud, or coercion
Custodial arrest or formal charging instrument filed for commercial sex trafficking resulting in a non-conviction outcome
Committing an act of sexual conduct by force or threat of force, or when the victim is unable to give consent
Any employee of a penal system, law enforcement agency, or county jail engaging in sexual penetration or sexual conduct with an individual in custody
Committing an act of oral or anal sexual penetration by the use of force or threat of force, or when the victim is unable to give consent
Committing an assault based on the location of the conduct, the status of the victim (e.g. peace officer teacher senior citizen) or through the deployment of a firearm device or motor vehicle
Custodial arrest or formal charging instrument filed for Aggravated Assault resulting in dismissal, acquittal, or dropped charges
Knowingly or intentionally without legal justification causing bodily harm or making physical contact of an insulting or provoking nature to any family or household member
Communicating a threat to another with the intent to cause that person or another to perform or omit the performance of any act
Knowingly without lawful authority detaining another person
Knowingly engaging in a course of conduct directed at a specific person, where the behavior would cause a reasonable person to suffer emotional distress or fear for bodily safety

Knowingly requiring the performance of an act by another for the purpose of induction or admission into any group or organization associated with an educational institution which results in bodily harm
Knowingly and secretly confining another person against their will or carrying another person by force or threat of force from one place to another with intent secretly to confine them
Custodial arrest or formal charging instrument filed for Kidnapping resulting in dismissal, acquittal, or dropped charges
Committing an assault against a family or household member as defined by the Illinois Domestic Violence Act
Committing an act of sexual penetration involving the anus or mouth by use of force or threat of force
Knowingly placing another person in reasonable apprehension of receiving a battery while using a deadly weapon or firearm
Custodial arrest or formal charging instrument filed for Aggravated Assault resulting in an acquittal, dismissal, or dropped charges
Knowingly or intentionally causing bodily harm to an individual or making physical contact of an insulting or provoking nature
Knowingly or intentionally causing bodily harm or making insulting or provoking contact with any family or household member
Knowingly communicating a threat to cause physical injury property damage or criminal accusations to compel another to perform or omit any act
Knowingly without legal authority detaining another person
Knowingly engaging in a course of conduct directed at a specific person that would cause a reasonable person to fear for their safety or suffer emotional distress
Knowingly requiring the performance of an act by another for initialization into any organization which causes bodily harm
Knowingly and secretly confining another person against their will or inducing another to go from one place to another by force or threat
Recklessly engaging in conduct which performs acts that endanger the bodily safety of an individual
Knowingly engaging in conduct which places another in reasonable apprehension of receiving a battery
Committing domestic battery by intentionally impeding the normal breathing or circulation of blood by applying pressure on the throat or neck
Transmitting a false report or threat concerning a bomb explosive compound or mass destruction mechanism
Committing battery while knowingly causing great bodily harm permanent disability or disfigurement
Knowingly and without authority entering or remaining within a building, watercraft, aircraft, or motor vehicle with intent to commit a felony or theft
Knowingly and without authority entering or remaining within the dwelling place of another with intent to commit a felony or theft
Knowingly entering the dwelling place of another without authority when one or more persons are present, and using or threatening force, or being armed with a dangerous weapon
Knowingly taking a motor vehicle from the person or the immediate presence of another by the use of force or by threatening the imminent use of force
Committing vehicular hijacking while armed with a dangerous weapon, or against a victim under 16, over 60, or a person with physical disabilities
Knowingly taking property from the person or presence of another by the use of force or by threatening the imminent use of force
Committing robbery while carrying or armed with a dangerous weapon other than a firearm or carrying an active firearm
Possessing cannabis or controlled substances without purchasing and affixing official tax stamps issued by the Department of Revenue
Knowingly delivering a controlled substance to a person under 18 years of age who is at least 3 years younger than the deliverer
Knowingly manufacturing, cultivating, producing, or delivering a controlled substance or counterfeit substance
Manufacturing or delivering a controlled substance within 500 feet of a school, public park, courthouse, or place of worship
Knowingly possessing a controlled substance, narcotic, or illicit chemical compound (excluding cannabis below specific thresholds)

Receiving income derived from a pattern of criminal gang activity or racketeering enterprise and using or investing any part of that income to acquire an interest in or operate real estate or commercial business operations
Knowingly receiving income from a pattern of streetgang activity and using or investing such income to acquire an interest in or establish an enterprise
Knowingly obtaining control over stolen property knowing the property to have been stolen or under such circumstances as would reasonably induce that belief
Knowingly obtaining control over stolen property knowing the property to have been stolen or under such circumstances as would reasonably induce belief that it was stolen
Knowingly obtaining or exerting unauthorized control over property of the owner with intent to deprive the owner permanently of the use or benefit of the property
Knowingly taking possession of, transferring, or altering price tags on merchandise displayed for sale in a retail establishment with intent to deprive the merchant permanently of the benefit
Direct or indirect acts committed which are calculated to embarrass hinder or obstruct a court in its administration of justice or derogate from its authority
Wilful conduct calculated to embarrass hinder or obstruct a court in its administration of justice or disrespect to the court's authority
Knowingly escaping from any penal institution or from the lawful custody of an officer after being convicted or charged with a felony or misdemeanor
Intentionally escaping from any penal institution or custodial custody after being convicted of a felony or charged with a felony
Knowingly communicating a threat of injury or damage to any witness or juror with intent to influence their testimony or verdict
Knowingly offering or paying any benefit to a witness, or threatening a witness, to influence testimony or induce absence from a legal proceeding
Knowingly resisting or obstructing the performance by one known to be a peace officer, firefighter, or correctional officer of any authorized act within their official capacity
Any driver of a motor vehicle who, having been given a visual or audible signal by a peace officer, wilfully flees or attempts to elude the officer
Knowingly making altering or issuing any document apparently capable of defrauding another such that it purports to have been made by another
Intentionally failing to surrender or appear before a court within 30 days of a missed mandatory trial date after being released on a felony charge under Pretrial Fairness Act guidelines
Knowingly and falsely representing oneself to be a peace officer, or using badges, uniforms, or vehicle modifications to project such authority
Knowingly soliciting another to blow into a BAIID, or tampering with, altering, or bypassing the device to start a motor vehicle
Agreeing with another person to commit an offense when an act in furtherance of such agreement is performed by any co-conspirator
Intent to commit a specific offense and performing any act constituting a substantial step toward the commission of that offense
Intent to commit a specific offense and agreeing with another to the commission of that offense followed by an overt act in furtherance of the agreement
Intent that an offense be committed and commanding, encouraging, or requesting another to commit that offense
Intent that the offense of First Degree Murder be committed and procuring another to commit that offense pursuant to an agreement for money or anything of value
With the intent that a felony be committed, commanding, encouraging, or requesting another to commit that offense
Knowingly engaging in the use of force or violence disturbing the public peace by 2 or more persons acting together without authority of law
Custodial arrest or formal charging instrument filed for Mob Action resulting in an acquittal, dismissal, or dropped charges

Committing any baseline predicate offense (assault, battery, theft, criminal damage to property) by reason of the actual or perceived race, color, creed, religion, ancestry, gender, sexual orientation, physical/mental disability, or national origin of another individual or group
Denying any person the full and equal enjoyment of the accommodations advantages facilities or privileges of any place of public accommodation based on prohibited discriminatory categories
Knowingly doing any act in such unreasonable manner as to alarm or disturb another and to provoke a breach of the peace
Intentionally or knowingly furnishing a false or fictitious name, residence address, or date of birth to a peace officer who has lawfully detained the person
Knowingly performing, offering, or agreeing to perform any act of sexual penetration or sexual conduct with another person for money or other valuable consideration
Custodial arrest or formal charging instrument filed for Prostitution resulting in an acquittal, dismissal, or dropped charges
Knowingly offering or agreeing to pay another person money or other valuable consideration to perform any act of sexual penetration or sexual conduct
Knowingly managing, supervising, controlling, or maintaining a prostitution enterprise, business, or place
Knowingly playing a game of chance or skill for money or other thing of value, or making a wager upon the result of any game, contest, or political election without state regulatory authorization
Conducting unauthorized pyrotechnic displays or distributing consumer fireworks without holding a valid state pyrotechnic distributor or operator license
Knowingly committing an act of cruelty to any animal, or causing, instigating, or permitting any torture, torment, or unjustifiable hardship upon an animal

Knowingly playing a game of chance or skill for money or other thing of value, or making a wager upon the result of any game, contest, or political nomination outside authorized state lotteries or licensed casino grids

A licensed concealed carry holder knowingly carrying a concealed firearm into a statutorily prohibited location (e.g. government facility, school, public transit vehicle, stadium, or establishment serving alcohol)
A minor under the age of 17 remaining in any public place street or commercial establishment during designated night hours without parent or lawful justification
Remaining in a public place with one or more persons known to be streetgang members under circumstances warranting a lawful dispersal order by police
Wilfully fleeing or attempting to elude a peace officer after being given a visual or audible signal, where the flight is at a rate of speed at least 21 miles per hour over the legal speed limit
Driving any vehicle with a wilful or wanton disregard for the safety of persons or property
Knowingly entering or remaining upon the land or inside the building of another after receiving notice before entry that such entry is forbidden
Carrying a concealed firearm after the structural Concealed Carry License has been revoked or suspended by official action of the Illinois State Police
Permitting a public nuisance or maintaining real property in a condition that violates municipal health and safety building codes
Operating a motor vehicle on a public highway when the driver's license or operating privilege has been suspended or revoked by the Secretary of State
Wilfully failing to immediately stop a motor vehicle at the scene of an accident causing damage to an attended vehicle, or to remain and exchange mandatory credentials
Wilfully fleeing or attempting to elude a peace officer after being given a visual or audible signal, where the flight is the proximate cause of bodily injury to any individual
Appearing in a public space under the influence of alcohol, narcotics, or other intoxicating compounds to a degree that endangers oneself, others, or creates an active public nuisance
Knowingly damaging by means of fire or explosive any real property or personal property having a value of $150 or more, without the consent of the owner

Knowingly damaging any structure by fire or explosive when the person knows or has reason to know that one or more persons are present, or when any person suffers great bodily harm or permanent disfigurement as a result
Knowingly damaging any property of another without their consent
Knowingly causing damage to the property of another without consent, where the monetary damage value exceeds $500 but does not exceed $10,000

Knowingly taking possession of, carrying away, or transferring any merchandise displayed or offered for sale in a retail establishment with intent to permanently deprive the merchant of its full value
Knowingly obtaining unauthorized control over the property of another with intent to permanently deprive, where the stolen property value exceeds $500 but does not exceed $10,000
Custodial arrest or formal charging instrument filed for felony-tier theft resulting in an acquittal dismissal or dropped charges
Knowingly obtaining the labor or services of another by deception force threat or ghost employment tokens with the intent to evade payment
Knowingly defacing any public or private property of another without their consent by the use of paint marking fluid ink or other etching mechanisms

Knowingly accessing a computer, network, or electronic data program without authorization, or exceeding authorized access to alter, damage, or delete data with intent to cause system operational disruptions
Knowingly using a counterfeit, stolen, altered, or revoked credit card or electronic access device to fraudulently obtain money, goods, services, or financial value
Knowingly issuing or delivering a check or draft upon a financial institution knowing that it will not be paid by the depository
Knowingly issuing or delivering a check or draft upon a financial institution knowing it will not be paid, where the face value of a single check or aggregated checks within 90 days exceeds $150
Knowingly making or causing to be made a false or fraudulent claim for payment or other benefit under an insurance policy

Knowingly conferring or offering to confer any benefit upon an employee, agent, or fiduciary without the consent of their principal, with intent to influence their professional conduct in relation to the principal's affairs

anchors.phrase: Knowingly engaging in a financial transaction using criminally derived property or proceeds of unlawful activity with the intent to promote the activity or conceal the nature, location, source, or ownership of the proceeds
Wilfully failing to file a return or filing a false fraudulent or intentionally altered return or document with the Illinois Department of Revenue to evade tax liabilities
Employing any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud in connection with the sale or purchase of securities, or making false statements or omissions of material facts to manipulate financial markets
Knowingly entering into any contract, combination, or conspiracy to fix, control, or maintain prices, or to rig bidding configurations for public or private market contracts
anchors.subcategory: Bribery of a Public Servant / Official

anchors.phrase: Managing or directing a corporate entity or partnership and knowingly executing fraudulent entries, false financial statements, or asset transfers to deceive shareholders, creditors, or regulators
Custodial arrest or formal charging instrument filed for corporate deception or asset manipulation resulting in an acquittal dismissal or dropped charges
Knowingly introducing malware, ransomware, or malicious encryption keys into a computer system or network and demanding money, property, or value to restore system access
Knowingly making false or misleading statements in any advertisement addressed to the public to promote the sale or distribution of property commodities or services
Knowingly executing a scheme to defraud a financial institution or to obtain money, assets, or securities under custody of a bank via fraudulent pretenses, statements, or misrepresentations
Knowingly executing a scheme to defraud or obtain money, property, or data by transmitting false representations, signals, or sounds over telephone lines, electronic wire networks, or media avenues
